explanation: Arjuna asked the Good Lord : O Lord KRISHNA, if you claim that Gyan or Knowledge is better than one’s Karma or responsibility, the why do you ask me to fight this battle?

sanskrit: व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे । तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम् ॥ ३.२ ॥
hindi: आप मिले हुए-से वचनोंसे मेरी बुद्धिको मानो मोहित कर रहे हैं इसलिये उस एक बातको निश्चित करके कहिये जिससे मैं कल्याणको प्राप्त हो जाऊँ|
english: I am confused dear Lord, by the advice you have given to me. You have told me to take two opposite and different courses of action at once. Please, O KRISHNA, tell me of just one wise solution and course of action that would lead out of this problem safely.
explanation: I am confused dear Lord, by the advice you have given to me. You have told me to take two opposite and different courses of action at once. Please, O KRISHNA, tell me of just one wise solution and course of action that would lead out of this problem safely.

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योग...
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले– हे निष्पाप ! इस लोकमें दो प्रकारकी निष्ठा’ मेरेद्वारा पहले कही गयी है । उनमेंसे सांख्ययोगियोंकी निष्ठा तो ज्ञानयोगसे और योगियोंकी निष्ठा कर्मयोगसे होती है|
english: The Blessed Lord replied : O Arjuna, always remember in life that there are only two definite paths of action as I have described before. One of these is known as Sankhyayoga or the path of knowledge, and the other is Karmayoga, or the path of performing duty and action without expecting any result.
explanation: The Blessed Lord replied : O Arjuna, always remember in life that there are only two definite paths of action as I have described before. One of these is known as Sankhyayoga or the path of knowledge, and the other is Karmayoga, or the path of performing duty and action without expecting any result.

sanskrit: न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते । न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥ ३.४ ॥
hindi: मनुष्य न तो कर्मोंका आरम्भ किये बिना निष्कर्मताको यानी योगनिष्ठाको प्राप्त होता है और न कर्मोंके केवल त्यागमात्र सिद्धि यानी सांख्यनिष्ठाको ही प्राप्त होता है|
english: O Arjuna, it is not only important to understand the meanings of Sankhyayoga and Karmayoga, but it is also important to understand the aims or goals behind each of these courses of action.The aim of Karmayoga is to achieve freedom from action. One cannot achieve the goal by total inaction or by not performing any action at all. The goal of Sankhyayoga is the realization of God. When one has fully realized God, he has reached a state of perfection. Once simply cannot attain this state of perfection by giving up or retreating from the performance of one’s actions.
explanation: O Arjuna, it is not only important to understand the meanings of Sankhyayoga and Karmayoga, but it is also important to understand the aims or goals behind each of these courses of action.The aim of Karmayoga is to achieve freedom from action. One cannot achieve the goal by total inaction or by not performing any action at all. The goal of Sankhyayoga is the realization of God. When one has fully realized God, he has reached a state of perfection. Once simply cannot attain this state of perfection by giving up or retreating from the performance of one’s actions.

sanskrit: न हि कश्चित्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत् । कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥ ३.५ ॥
hindi: निःसन्देह कोई भी मनुष्य किसी भी कालमें क्षणमात्र भी बिना कर्म किये नहीं रहता; क्योंकि सारा मनुष्यसमुदाय प्रकृतिजनित गुणोंद्वारा परवश हुआ कर्म करनेके लिये बाध्य किया जाता है|
english: Lord Krishna continued:There is nobody who is living that does not perform an action of some sort even for a moment. Everyone is forced to perform actions by natural tendencies (human nature).
explanation: Lord Krishna continued:There is nobody who is living that does not perform an action of some sort even for a moment. Everyone is forced to perform actions by natural tendencies (human nature).

sanskrit: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् । इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥ ३.६ ॥
hindi: जो मूढ़बुद्धि मनुष्य समस्त इन्द्रियोंको हठपूर्वक ऊपरसे रोककर मनसे उन इन्द्रियोंके विषयोंका चिन्तन करता रहता है, वह मिथ्याचारी अर्थात् दम्भी कहा जाता है|
english: O Arjuna, those who have learned to control the organs of action, yet still think in their minds of the pleasures that they can get from those organs, are fooling only themselves by pretending to have certain qualities that do not really exist within them.
explanation: O Arjuna, those who have learned to control the organs of action, yet still think in their minds of the pleasures that they can get from those organs, are fooling only themselves by pretending to have certain qualities that do not really exist within them.

sanskrit: यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन । कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥ ३.७ ॥
hindi: किन्तु हे अर्जुन ! जो पुरुष मनसे इन्द्रियोंको वशमें करके अनासक्त हुआ समस्त इन्द्रियों द्वारा कर्मयोगका आचरण करता है, वही श्रेष्ठ है|
english: The Blessed Lord spoke: O Arjuna, one who has fully learned to control his senses with his mind and practises selfless Karmayoga keeping his senses controlled and not allowing them to interfere and disrupt his action, is truly a great person.
explanation: The Blessed Lord spoke: O Arjuna, one who has fully learned to control his senses with his mind and practises selfless Karmayoga keeping his senses controlled and not allowing them to interfere and disrupt his action, is truly a great person.

sanskrit: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः । शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥ ३.८ ॥
hindi: तू शास्त्रविहित कर्तव्यकर्म कर; क्योंकि कर्म न करनेकी अपेक्षा कर्म करना श्रेष्ठ है तथा कर्म न करनेसे तेरा शरीर- निर्वाह भी नहीं सिद्ध होगा|
english: Perform the actions that you have been obliged to perform, or that have been prescribed for you. Action is always better than inaction, If one is inactive, he cannot live, simply because he is not performing the action of maintaining his body.
explanation: Perform the actions that you have been obliged to perform, or that have been prescribed for you. Action is always better than inaction, If one is inactive, he cannot live, simply because he is not performing the action of maintaining his body.

sanskrit: यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः । तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्गः समाचर ॥ ३.९ ॥
hindi: यज्ञके निमित्त किये जानेवाले कर्मोंसे अतिरिक्त दूसरे कर्मोंमें लगा हुआ ही यह मनुष्यसमुदाय कर्मोंसे बँधता है। इसलिये हे अर्जुन! तू आसक्तिसे रहित होकर उस यज्ञके निमित्त ही भलीभाँति कर्तव्यकर्म कर|
english: The Lord continued: O Arjuna, all the actions that a person performs in this world, except of course those associated with sacrifice or Yagya, tie that person to the world. Therefore, O Arjuna to break free from this link, perform all your actions well without being attached to them.
explanation: The Lord continued: O Arjuna, all the actions that a person performs in this world, except of course those associated with sacrifice or Yagya, tie that person to the world. Therefore, O Arjuna to break free from this link, perform all your actions well without being attached to them.

sanskrit: देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः । परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ ॥ ३.११ ॥
hindi: तुमलोग इस यज्ञके द्वारा देवताओंको उन्नत करो और वे देवता तुमलोगोंको उन्नत करें। इस प्रकार निःस्वार्थभावसे एक-दूसरेको उन्नत करते हुए तुमलोग परम कल्याणको प्राप्त हो जाओगे|
english: The Lord said unto Arjuna: The Gods or Deities grow with Yagya or sacrifice. When the Deities grow, they will help you to grow. Thus, both Deity and mankind grow continually. they will both achieve their supreme goal.
explanation: The Lord said unto Arjuna: The Gods or Deities grow with Yagya or sacrifice. When the Deities grow, they will help you to grow. Thus, both Deity and mankind grow continually. they will both achieve their supreme goal.

sanskrit: इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः । तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः ॥ ३.१...
hindi: यज्ञके द्वारा बढ़ाये हुए देवता तुमलोगोंको बिना माँगे ही इच्छित भोग निश्चय ही देते रहेंगे । इस प्रकार उन देवताओंके द्वारा दिये हुए भोगोंको जो पुरुष उनको बिना दिये स्वयं भोगता है, वह चोर ही है|
english: Through the spirit of sacrifice of Yagya, the Deities grow and progress. As they grow, they will provide you with all the pleasures you desire. You should offer part of these pleasures to the Deities.If one is granted pleasures by another person, the one who receives the pleasures should share some of those pleasures with the provider of those pleasures, or else he is as bad as a thief.
explanation: Through the spirit of sacrifice of Yagya, the Deities grow and progress. As they grow, they will provide you with all the pleasures you desire. You should offer part of these pleasures to the Deities.If one is granted pleasures by another person, the one who receives the pleasures should share some of those pleasures with the provider of those pleasures, or else he is as bad as a thief.

sanskrit: यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः । भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥ ३.१३ ॥
hindi: यज्ञसे बचे हुए अन्नको खानेवाले श्रेष्ठ पुरुष सब पापोंसे मुक्त हो जाते हैं और जो पापीलोग अपना शरीर पोषण करनेके लिये ही अन्न पकाते – हैं, वे तो पापको ही खाते हैं|
english: The Blessed Lord Krishna said:People who eat food after offering it for sacrifice are considered pious, pure, and are freed from all sins. People who prepare food only for themselves commit sins and are impure.
explanation: The Blessed Lord Krishna said:People who eat food after offering it for sacrifice are considered pious, pure, and are freed from all sins. People who prepare food only for themselves commit sins and are impure.

sanskrit: अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः । यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः ॥ ३.१४ ॥
hindi: सम्पूर्ण प्राणी अन्नसे उत्पन्न होते हैं, अन्नकी उत्पत्ति वृष्टिसे होती है, वृष्टि यज्ञसे होती है और यज्ञ विहित कर्मोंसे उत्पन्न होनेवाला है। कर्मसमुदायको तू वेदसे उत्पन्न और वेदको अविनाशी परमात्मासे उत्पन्न हुआ जान। इससे सिद्ध होता है कि सर्वव्यापी परम अक्षर परमात्मा सदा ही यज्ञमें प्रतिष्ठित है|
english: Food allows people to live. Food is produced from rain. Rain arises from sacrifice. Actions produce sacrifice or Yagna. God produces knowledge. Knowledge produces actions. Actions produce sacrifice or Yagya. Where there is sacrifice, the omnipresent God is there also.
explanation: Food allows people to live. Food is produced from rain. Rain arises from sacrifice. Actions produce sacrifice or Yagna. God produces knowledge. Knowledge produces actions. Actions produce sacrifice or Yagya. Where there is sacrifice, the omnipresent God is there also.

sanskrit: कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम् । तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥ ३...
hindi: सम्पूर्ण प्राणी अन्नसे उत्पन्न होते हैं, अन्नकी उत्पत्ति वृष्टिसे होती है, वृष्टि यज्ञसे होती है और यज्ञ विहित कर्मोंसे उत्पन्न होनेवाला है। कर्मसमुदायको तू वेदसे उत्पन्न और वेदको अविनाशी परमात्मासे उत्पन्न हुआ जान। इससे सिद्ध होता है कि सर्वव्यापी परम अक्षर परमात्मा सदा ही यज्ञमें प्रतिष्ठित है|
english: Food allows people to live. Food is produced from rain. Rain arises from sacrifice. Actions produce sacrifice or Yagna.God produces knowledge. Knowledge produces actions. Actions produce sacrifice or Yagya.Where there is sacrifice, the omnipresent God is there also.
explanation: Food allows people to live. Food is produced from rain. Rain arises from sacrifice. Actions produce sacrifice or Yagna.God produces knowledge. Knowledge produces actions. Actions produce sacrifice or Yagya.Where there is sacrifice, the omnipresent God is there also.

sanskrit: एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः । अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥ ३.१६ ॥
hindi: हे पार्थ! जो पुरुष इस लोकमें इस प्रकार परम्परासे प्रचलित सृष्टिचक्रके अनुकूल नहीं बरतता अर्थात् अपने कर्तव्यका पालन नहीं करता, वह इन्द्रियों के द्वारा भोगोंमें रमण करनेवाला पापायु पुरुष व्यर्थ ही जीता है|
english: The Blessed Lord said: O Arjuna, he who does not follow the proper course of creation such as that I have just described, but centres his life around the enjoyment of luxuries, this sinful person, in my eyes, leads a useless life. He shall never fully understand and realize me.
explanation: The Blessed Lord said: O Arjuna, he who does not follow the proper course of creation such as that I have just described, but centres his life around the enjoyment of luxuries, this sinful person, in my eyes, leads a useless life. He shall never fully understand and realize me.

sanskrit: यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः । आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते ॥ ३.१७ ॥
hindi: परन्तु जो मनुष्य आत्मामें ही रमण करनेवाला और आत्मामें ही तृप्त तथा आत्मामें ही सन्तुष्ट हो, उसके लिये कोई कर्तव्य नहीं है|
english: He, O Arjuna, who is satisfied and content in himself, and he who is absorbed in himself, actions and duties do not exist (for him).
explanation: He, O Arjuna, who is satisfied and content in himself, and he who is absorbed in himself, actions and duties do not exist (for him).

sanskrit: नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन । न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्चिदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥ ३.१८ ॥
hindi: उस महापुरुषका इस विश्वमें न तो कर्म करनेसे कोई प्रयोजन रहता है और न कर्मोंके न करनेसे ही कोई प्रयोजन रहता है तथा सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें । भी इसका किञ्चिन्मात्र भी स्वार्थका सम्बन्ध नहीं रहता|
english: This type of person has no use for actions (duties performed for the attainment of a certain goal), or inactions (duties performed without the expectation of any results).This type of person has reached a very high stage in the attainment of peace and detachment from all beings and things. He no longer selfishly depends on anybody or anything.
explanation: This type of person has no use for actions (duties performed for the attainment of a certain goal), or inactions (duties performed without the expectation of any results).This type of person has reached a very high stage in the attainment of peace and detachment from all beings and things. He no longer selfishly depends on anybody or anything.

sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर । असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः ॥ ३.१९ ॥
hindi: इसलिये तू निरन्तर आसक्तिसे रहित होकर सदा कर्तव्यकर्मको भलीभाँति करता रह क्योंकि आसक्तिसे रहित होकर कर्म करता हुआ मनुष्य परमात्माको प्राप्त हो जाता है|
english: Therefore, Arjuna always perform your given duties without feelings of attachment towards any being or anything. A person doing unattached actions, or in other words, “inction” attains the Lord, the key to perfection.
explanation: Therefore, Arjuna always perform your given duties without feelings of attachment towards any being or anything. A person doing unattached actions, or in other words, “inction” attains the Lord, the key to perfection.

sanskrit: यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः । स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥ ३.२१ ॥
hindi: श्रेष्ठ पुरुष जो जो आचरण करता है, अन्य पुरुष भी वैसा – वैसा ही आचरण करते हैं । वह जो कुछ प्रमाण कर देता है, समस्त मनुष्यसमुदाय उसीके अनुसार बरतने लग जाता है|
english: If a great man sets an example for the world, the world will follow him. Whatever standards or values he sets, people generally will follow the same set of standards and values.
explanation: If a great man sets an example for the world, the world will follow him. Whatever standards or values he sets, people generally will follow the same set of standards and values.

sanskrit: न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किंचन । नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥ ३.२२ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! मुझे इन तीनों लोकोंमें न तो कुछ कर्तव्य है और न कोई भी प्राप्त करनेयोग्य वस्तु अप्राप्त है, तो भी मैं कर्ममें ही बरतता हूँ|
english: The Blessed Lord spoke: O Arjuna, take Me for example. There is nothing that is not available for me in this universe, nor is there any specific function or duty for me to perform, still, however, I perform Karma.
explanation: The Blessed Lord spoke: O Arjuna, take Me for example. There is nothing that is not available for me in this universe, nor is there any specific function or duty for me to perform, still, however, I perform Karma.

sanskrit: यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः । मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥ ३.२३ ॥
hindi: क्योंकि हे पार्थ! यदि कदाचित् मैं सावधान होकर कर्मोंमें न बरतँ तो बड़ी हानि हो जाय; क्योंकि मनुष्य सब प्रकारसे मेरे ही मार्गका अनुसरण करते हैं|
english: O Arjuna, if I do not perform my duties, the destruction of all regions of the universe will come about. Mixed castes will develop and all the beings on the face of this earth will be destroyed.
explanation: O Arjuna, if I do not perform my duties, the destruction of all regions of the universe will come about. Mixed castes will develop and all the beings on the face of this earth will be destroyed.

sanskrit: उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम् । संकरस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥ ३.२४ ॥
hindi: इसलिये यदि मैं कर्म न करूँ तो ये सब मनुष्य न नष्ट-भ्रष्ट हो जायँ और मैं संकरताका करनेवाला होऊँ तथा इस समस्त प्रजाको नष्ट करनेवाला बनूँ|
english: These worlds would if I did no do action, I would be the cause of confusion of castes and I would destroy these beings.
explanation: These worlds would if I did no do action, I would be the cause of confusion of castes and I would destroy these beings.

sanskrit: सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत । कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्चिकीर्षुर्लोकसंग्रहम् ॥ ३.२५...
hindi: हे भारत ! कर्ममें आसक्त हुए अज्ञानीजन जिस प्रकार कर्म करते हैं, आसक्तिरहित विद्वान् भी लोकसंग्रह करना चाहता हुआ उसी प्रकार कर्म करे|
english: The Blessed Lord said: Just as the ignorant perform actions with attachment to things and/or beings, so should the wise men take action for the preservation of world order, without developing any attachments whatsoever, O Arjuna.
explanation: The Blessed Lord said: Just as the ignorant perform actions with attachment to things and/or beings, so should the wise men take action for the preservation of world order, without developing any attachments whatsoever, O Arjuna.

sanskrit: न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम् । जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान्युक्तः समाचरन् ॥ ३.२६ ॥
hindi: परमात्माके स्वरूपमें अटल स्थित हुए ज्ञानी पुरुषको चाहिये कि वह शास्त्रविहित कर्मोंमें आसक्तिवाले अज्ञानियोंकी बुद्धिमें भ्रम अर्थात् कर्मोंमें अश्रद्धा उत्पन्न न करे । किन्तु स्वयं शास्त्रविहित समस्त कर्म भलीभाँति करता हुआ उनसे भी वैसे ही करवावे|
english: It is not a wise man’s concern or responsibility to fill the ignorant person’s mind with doubts, even if the latter is attached to his actions and constantly awaits results.However, he should encourafe these ignorant people just as any great man, by performing his own duties and actions unattached to them, as well as possible.
explanation: It is not a wise man’s concern or responsibility to fill the ignorant person’s mind with doubts, even if the latter is attached to his actions and constantly awaits results.However, he should encourafe these ignorant people just as any great man, by performing his own duties and actions unattached to them, as well as possible.

sanskrit: प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः । अहंकारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते ॥ ३.२७ ॥
hindi: वास्तवमें सम्पूर्ण कर्म सब प्रकारसे प्रकृतिके गुणोंद्वारा किये जाते हैं तो भी जिसका अन्तःकरण अहंकारसे मोहित हो रहा है, ऐसा अज्ञानी मैं कर्ता हूँ’ ऐसा मानता है|
english: The Blessed Lord Krishna spoke: O Arjuna, all actions that are performed by beings, are done so by three modes of Prakrithi or three aspects of nature. However, ignorant people claim themselves as the performer of their actions.
explanation: The Blessed Lord Krishna spoke: O Arjuna, all actions that are performed by beings, are done so by three modes of Prakrithi or three aspects of nature. However, ignorant people claim themselves as the performer of their actions.

sanskrit: तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः । गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते ॥ ३.२८ ॥
hindi: परन्तु हे महाबाहो ! गुणविभाग और कर्मविभाग के तत्त्व को जाननेवाला ज्ञानयोगी सम्पूर्ण गुण ही गुणोंमें बरत रहे हैं, ऐसा समझकर उनमें आसक्त नहीं होता|
english: He who understands fully, O Arjuna, the objects of perception (senses, mind, etc.), divisions of Prakrithi (nature), and selfless action, has the ability to understand the relationship of senses and sensual objects and never gets attached to any beings or objects.
explanation: He who understands fully, O Arjuna, the objects of perception (senses, mind, etc.), divisions of Prakrithi (nature), and selfless action, has the ability to understand the relationship of senses and sensual objects and never gets attached to any beings or objects.

sanskrit: प्रकृतेर्गुणसंमूढाः सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु । तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत् ॥ ३.२९ ॥
hindi: प्रकृतिके गुणोंसे अत्यन्त मोहित हुए मनुष्य गुणोंमें और कर्मोंमें आसक्त रहते हैं, उन पूर्णतया न समझनेवाले मन्दबुद्धि अज्ञानियोंको पूर्णतया जाननेवाला ज्ञानी विचलित न करे|
english: The Lord spoke: Those who are deceived by the Gunas of nature or Prakrithi and are deceived by karma, develop an attachment to the Gunas and to their actions.A wise person would not disturb, or have anything to do with these ignorant beings, O Arjuna.
explanation: The Lord spoke: Those who are deceived by the Gunas of nature or Prakrithi and are deceived by karma, develop an attachment to the Gunas and to their actions.A wise person would not disturb, or have anything to do with these ignorant beings, O Arjuna.

sanskrit: ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः । श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः ॥ ३.३१ ॥
hindi: जो कोई मनुष्य दोषदृष्टिसे रहित और श्रद्धायुक्त होकर मेरे इस मतका सदा अनुसरण करते हैं, वे भी सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंसे छूट जाते हैं|
english: Those wise people, with faith in Me, and those who follow my teaching, are always given freedom from their actions (Karma).
explanation: Those wise people, with faith in Me, and those who follow my teaching, are always given freedom from their actions (Karma).

sanskrit: ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम् । सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः ॥ ३.३२ ॥
hindi: परन्तु जो मनुष्य मुझमें दोषारोपण करते हुए मेरे इस मतके अनुसार नहीं चलते हैं, उन मूर्खोको तू सम्पूर्ण ज्ञानोंमें मोहित और नष्ट हुए ही समझ|
english: On the other hand, O Arjuna, those of poor intelligence that do not follow my teaching are ignorant; regard them as mere fools.
explanation: On the other hand, O Arjuna, those of poor intelligence that do not follow my teaching are ignorant; regard them as mere fools.

sanskrit: सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि । प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति ॥ ३.३३ ॥
hindi: सभी प्राणी प्रकृतिको प्राप्त होते हैं अर्थात् अपने स्वभावके परवश हुए कर्म करते हैं। ज्ञानवान् भी अपनी प्रकृतिके अनुसार चेष्टा करता है। फिर इसमें किसीका हठ क्या करेगा ?
english: All beings, wise or unwise, are forced to act by nature. What can restraint possibly do, O Arjuna?
explanation: All beings, wise or unwise, are forced to act by nature. What can restraint possibly do, O Arjuna?

sanskrit: इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ । तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ ॥ ३.३४ ॥
hindi: इन्द्रिय इन्द्रियके अर्थमें अर्थात् प्रत्येक इन्द्रियके विषयमें राग और द्वेष छिपे हुए स्थित हैं। मनुष्यको उन दोनोंके वशमें नहीं होना चाहिये, क्योंकि वे दोनों ही इसके कल्याणमार्गमें विघ्न करनेवाले हैं महान् शत्रु है|
english: The Divine Lord stated:The enjoyment of sensual objects by their senses (an example of human nature) creates barriers to the path of Bliss and peace if one becomes a victim of attachment to his sesses.
explanation: The Divine Lord stated:The enjoyment of sensual objects by their senses (an example of human nature) creates barriers to the path of Bliss and peace if one becomes a victim of attachment to his sesses.

sanskrit: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् । स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः ॥ ३.३५ ॥
hindi: अच्छी प्रकार आचरणमें लाये हुए दूसरेके धर्मसे गुणरहित भी अपना धर्म अति उत्तम है। अपने धर्ममें तो मरना भी कल्याणकारक है और दूसरेका धर्म भयको देनेवाला है|
english: One’s own duty (Dharma) is more favourable than the well-established duty of others. To even encounter death, while performing one’s own duties (Dharma), is truly divine. However another person’s duty is filles with menace and fear.
explanation: One’s own duty (Dharma) is more favourable than the well-established duty of others. To even encounter death, while performing one’s own duties (Dharma), is truly divine. However another person’s duty is filles with menace and fear.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच । अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः ॥ ३.३६ ॥
hindi: अर्जुन बोले – हे कृष्ण ! तो फिर यह मनुष्य स्वयं – न चाहता हुआ भी बलात् लगाये हुएकी भाँति किससे प्रेरित होकर पापका आचरण करता है ?
english: Arjuna asked the Lord: O Lord Krishna, what motivates a person to commit sins that were committed involuntarily or by the force of others?
explanation: Arjuna asked the Lord: O Lord Krishna, what motivates a person to commit sins that were committed involuntarily or by the force of others?

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥ ३.३७ ॥
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले– रजोगुणसे उत्पन्न हुआ यह काम ही क्रोध है, यह बहुत खानेवाला अर्थात् भोगोंसे कभी न अघानेवाला और बड़ा पापी है, इसको ही तू इस विषयमें वैरी जान|
english: The Divine Lord replied: It is desire and wrath or anger arising from the evil (Rajasik) Guna in the form of the great fire of attachment. In this case, consider this fire the enemy and the sinner.
explanation: The Divine Lord replied: It is desire and wrath or anger arising from the evil (Rajasik) Guna in the form of the great fire of attachment. In this case, consider this fire the enemy and the sinner.

sanskrit: धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथादर्शो मलेन च । यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम् ॥ ३.३८ ॥
hindi: जिस प्रकार धूऍसे अग्नि और मैलसे दर्पण ढका जाता है तथा जिस प्रकार जेरसे गर्भ ढका रहता है, वैसे ही उस कामके द्वारा यह ज्ञान ढका रहता है|
english: Just as the smoke surrounds and covers a fire, just as dust surrounds and covers a mirror, and just as the amnion covers the embryo, similarly. Gyan or knowledge is covered and surrounded by one’s desires. O Arjuna.
explanation: Just as the smoke surrounds and covers a fire, just as dust surrounds and covers a mirror, and just as the amnion covers the embryo, similarly. Gyan or knowledge is covered and surrounded by one’s desires. O Arjuna.

sanskrit: आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा । कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च ॥ ३.३९ ॥
hindi: और हे अर्जुन! इस अग्निके समान कभी न पूर्ण होनेवाले कामरूप ज्ञानियोंके नित्य वैरीके द्वारा मनुष्यका ज्ञान ढका हुआ है|
english: The wise men, O Arjuna, have one constant enemy, namely, the unending fire of desire which covers the Gyan or Knowledge of the wise.
explanation: The wise men, O Arjuna, have one constant enemy, namely, the unending fire of desire which covers the Gyan or Knowledge of the wise.

sanskrit: तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ । पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम् ॥ ३.४१ ॥
hindi: इसलिये हे अर्जुन! तू पहले इन्द्रियों को वशमें करके इस ज्ञान और विज्ञानका नाश करनेवाले महान् पापी कामको अवश्य ही बलपूर्वक मार डाल|
english: The Blessed Lord advised: Therefore, O Arjuna, restrain the senses first and control your sinful desires, the enemy of Gyan or knowledge.
explanation: The Blessed Lord advised: Therefore, O Arjuna, restrain the senses first and control your sinful desires, the enemy of Gyan or knowledge.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः । मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः ॥ ३.४२ ॥
hindi: इन्द्रियोंको स्थूल शरीरसे पर यानी श्रेष्ठ, बलवान् और सूक्ष्म कहते हैं; इन इन्द्रियोंसे पर मन है, मनसे भी पर बुद्धि है और जो बुद्धिसे भी अत्यन्त पर है वह आत्मा है|
english: O Arjuna, never understimate the senses for they are very powerful. The mind is stronger than the senses however, O Arjuna. The intellect is even stronger than one’s mind and the soul is still stronger than one’s intellect.
explanation: O Arjuna, never understimate the senses for they are very powerful. The mind is stronger than the senses however, O Arjuna. The intellect is even stronger than one’s mind and the soul is still stronger than one’s intellect.

sanskrit: एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना । जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम् ॥ ३.४३ ॥
hindi: इस प्रकार बुद्धिसे पर अर्थात् सूक्ष्म, बलवान् और अत्यन्त श्रेष्ठ आत्माको जानकर और बुद्धि के द्वारा मनको वशमें करके हे महाबाहो ! तू इस कामरूप दुर्जय शत्रुको मार डाल|
english: Therefore, Oh Arjuna, knowing that your soul is stronger than your intellect, and being aware that the intellect controls the mind, destroy that invincible and dominating enemy known as desire.
explanation: Therefore, Oh Arjuna, knowing that your soul is stronger than your intellect, and being aware that the intellect controls the mind, destroy that invincible and dominating enemy known as desire.

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम् विवस्वान्मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत् ॥ ...
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले– मैंने इस अविनाशी योगको सूर्यसे कहा थ; सूर्यने अपने पुत्र वैवस्वत मनुसे कहा और मनुने अपने पुत्र राजा इक्ष्वाकुसे कहा|
english: Lord Krishna continued: I taught this immortal, everlasting “Yoga faction” (Karmayoga) to the Sun God Vivaswan.Vivaswan taught this knowledge to his son Manu and Manu taught this knowledge to his son Ikswaku.
explanation: Lord Krishna continued: I taught this immortal, everlasting “Yoga faction” (Karmayoga) to the Sun God Vivaswan.Vivaswan taught this knowledge to his son Manu and Manu taught this knowledge to his son Ikswaku.

sanskrit: एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः । स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्टः परन्तप ॥ ४.२ ॥
hindi: हे परंतप अर्जुन ! इस प्रकार परम्परासे प्राप्त इस योगको राजर्षियोंने जाना; किन्तु उसके बाद वह योग बहुत कालसे इस पृथ्वीलोकमें लुप्तप्राय हो गया|
english: By handing down this knowledge of Yoga from generation to generation this knowledge slowly deteriorated, and finally disappeared.
explanation: By handing down this knowledge of Yoga from generation to generation this knowledge slowly deteriorated, and finally disappeared.

sanskrit: स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः । भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् ॥ ४.३ ॥
hindi: तू मेरा भक्त और प्रिय सखा है, इसलिये वही यह पुरातन योग आज मैंने तुझको कहा है; क्योंकि यह बड़ा ही उत्तम रहस्य है अर्थात् गुप्त रखनेयोग्य विषय है|
english: O Arjuna, I reveal this ancient and most important secret of Yoga unto you because you are My dear devotee and friend.
explanation: O Arjuna, I reveal this ancient and most important secret of Yoga unto you because you are My dear devotee and friend.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच । अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति ॥ ४.४ ॥
hindi: अर्जुन बोले—आपका जन्म तो अर्वाचीन अभी हालका है और सूर्यका जन्म बहुत पुराना है अर्थात् कल्पके आदिमें हो चुका था । तब मैं इस बातको कैसे समझँ कि आपहीने कल्पके आदिमें सूर्यसे यह योग कहा था?
english: Arjuna asked Lord Krishna: Dear Lord, your birth on earth is very recent. The Sun however, took birth an extremely long time ago. I find it difficult then to understand how you could have told this ancient secret to Vivaswan the Sun God.
explanation: Arjuna asked Lord Krishna: Dear Lord, your birth on earth is very recent. The Sun however, took birth an extremely long time ago. I find it difficult then to understand how you could have told this ancient secret to Vivaswan the Sun God.

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप ॥ ४....
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले-हे परंतप अर्जुन ! मेरे और तेरे बहुत-से जन्म हो चुके हैं। उन सबको तू नहीं जानता, किन्तु मैं जानता हूँ|
english: The Lord looked upon Arjuna and replied: Arjuna, the answer to your question is simple. You are forgetting, O Son of Kunti, the both you and I have taken many births in this world together. However, only I, Lord, can remember this; you cannot.
explanation: The Lord looked upon Arjuna and replied: Arjuna, the answer to your question is simple. You are forgetting, O Son of Kunti, the both you and I have taken many births in this world together. However, only I, Lord, can remember this; you cannot.

sanskrit: अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन् । प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय संभवाम्यात्ममायया ॥ ४.६ ॥
hindi: मैं अजन्मा और अविनाशीस्वरूप होते हुए भी तथा समस्त प्राणियोंका ईश्वर होते हुए भी अपनी प्रकृतिको अधीन करके अपनी योगमायासे प्रकट होता हूँ|
english: O Arjuna, although birth and death do not exist for Me, since I am the immortal Lord, I appear on earth, by my Divine powers and ability, keeping My nature (Maya) under control.
explanation: O Arjuna, although birth and death do not exist for Me, since I am the immortal Lord, I appear on earth, by my Divine powers and ability, keeping My nature (Maya) under control.

sanskrit: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत । अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥ ४.७ ॥
hindi: हे भारत ! जब-जब धर्मकी हानि और अधर्मकी वृद्धि होती है, तब-तब ही मैं अपने रूपको रचता हूँ अर्थात् साकाररूपसे लोगोंके सम्मुख प्रकट होता हूँ|
english: The Lord declared solemnly unto Arjuna: O Arjuna, whenever good is overcome by evil, or whenever evil manifests itself throughout the world in such a way that good no longer exists. I will always appear on earth.
explanation: The Lord declared solemnly unto Arjuna: O Arjuna, whenever good is overcome by evil, or whenever evil manifests itself throughout the world in such a way that good no longer exists. I will always appear on earth.

sanskrit: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् । धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे ॥ ४.८ ॥
hindi: साधु पुरुषोंका उद्धार करनेके लिये, पापकर्म करनेवालोंका विनाश करनेके लिये और धर्मकी अच्छी तरहसे स्थापना करनेके लिये मैं युग-युगमें प्रकट हुआ करता हूँ|
english:
O Arjuna, I appear on earth from time to time for three main purposes. The first is for the protection and preservation of all that represents good, and of all those pure and saintly people on earth. The second purpose for which I appear on earth is for the destruction and removed and wrong-doers.
The third purpose is, of course, for the establishment and creation (or recreation) of Dharma (Righteousness or reinstating moral values among people on earth.)
explanation:
O Arjuna, I appear on earth from time to time for three main purposes. The first is for the protection and preservation of all that represents good, and of all those pure and saintly people on earth. The second purpose for which I appear on earth is for the destruction and removed and wrong-doers.
The third purpose is, of course, for the establishment and creation (or recreation) of Dharma (Righteousness or reinstating moral values among people on earth.)

sanskrit: जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः । त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥ ४.९ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! मेरे जन्म और कर्म दिव्य अर्थात् निर्मल और अलौकिक हैं – इस प्रकार जो मनुष्य तत्त्वसे – जान लेता है, वह शरीरको त्यागकर फिर जन्मको प्राप्त नहीं होता, किन्तु मुझे ही प्राप्त होता है|
english: O Arjuna, I was born divinely, all my action (Karma) are divine. The one who knows and understands this fully in reality, is not born again after leaving the body. He comes to me in Heaven for eternity.
explanation: O Arjuna, I was born divinely, all my action (Karma) are divine. The one who knows and understands this fully in reality, is not born again after leaving the body. He comes to me in Heaven for eternity.

sanskrit: ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम् । मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥ ४.११ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! जो भक्त मुझे जिस प्रकार भजते हैं, मैं भी उनको उसी प्रकार भजता हूँ; क्योंकि सभी मनुष्य सब प्रकारसे मेरे ही मार्गका अनुसरण करते हैं|
english: O Arjuna, those people who love, trust and worship Me receive the same love, trust and worship from Me. All wise men follow Me in all respects. They follow my every path.
explanation: O Arjuna, those people who love, trust and worship Me receive the same love, trust and worship from Me. All wise men follow Me in all respects. They follow my every path.

sanskrit: काङ्क्षन्तः कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवताः । क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा ॥ ४.१२ ॥
hindi: इस मनुष्यलोकमें कर्मोंके फलको चाहनेवाले लोग देवताओंका पूजन किया करते हैं; क्योंकि उनको कर्मोंसे उत्पन्न होनेवाली सिद्धि शीघ्र मिल जाती है|
english: O Arjuna, those who do actions or Karma for the fulfillment of their desires and goals and those who worship deities (gods and god desses) for their prosperity, they will undobtedly achieve success quickly.
explanation: O Arjuna, those who do actions or Karma for the fulfillment of their desires and goals and those who worship deities (gods and god desses) for their prosperity, they will undobtedly achieve success quickly.

sanskrit: चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः । तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् ॥ ४.१३ ॥
hindi: चार गुना-जाति मेरे द्वारा गुना और डी कर्मा के भेदभाव के अनुसार बनाई गई है; यद्यपि मैं इसके लेखक हूँ, मुझे गैर-कर्ता और अपरिवर्तनीय के रूप में जानते हैं।
english: The fourfold-caste has been created by Me according to the differentiation of GUNA an d KARMA; though I am the author thereof know Me as non-doer and immutable.
explanation: The fourfold-caste has been created by Me according to the differentiation of GUNA an d KARMA; though I am the author thereof know Me as non-doer and immutable.

sanskrit: न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा । इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते ॥ ४.१४ ॥
hindi: कर्मोंके फलमें मेरी स्पृहा नहीं है, इसलिये मुझे कर्म लिप्त नहीं करते – इस प्रकार जो मुझे तत्त्वसे जान लेता है, वह भी कर्मोंसे नहीं बँधता|
english: The Lord continued: O Arjuna, since I, the immortal Lord, have no cravings for the fruits of action, I do not desire the reward of Karma. Therefore those who know me well are not affected or bound by Karma (action).
explanation: The Lord continued: O Arjuna, since I, the immortal Lord, have no cravings for the fruits of action, I do not desire the reward of Karma. Therefore those who know me well are not affected or bound by Karma (action).

sanskrit: एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः । कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम् ॥ ४.१...
hindi: पूर्वकालमें मुमुक्षुओं ने भी इस प्रकार जानकर ही कर्म किये हैं। इसलिये तू भी पूर्वजोंद्वारा सदासे किये जानेवाले कर्मोंको ही कर|
english: The Lord continued:O Arjuna, ancient wise men who were searching for salvation in me, knew the facts well. However they even performed actions. Therefore O Arjuna, continue performing your actions doing your Karma as your ancestors once did in the past.
explanation: The Lord continued:O Arjuna, ancient wise men who were searching for salvation in me, knew the facts well. However they even performed actions. Therefore O Arjuna, continue performing your actions doing your Karma as your ancestors once did in the past.

sanskrit: किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः । तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥ ४....
hindi: कर्म क्या है ? और अकर्म क्या है ? – इस प्रकार इसका निर्णय करनेमें बुद्धिमान् पुरुष भी मोहित हो जाते हैं। इसलिये वह कर्मतत्त्व मैं तुझे भलीभाँति समझाकर कहूँगा, जिसे जानकर तू अशुभसे अर्थात् कर्मबन्धनसे मुक्त हो जायगा|
english: Even the wise men are confused about Karma (action) and Akarma (inaction). I shall now explain to you the truth about Karma. Knowing the truth, O Arjuna, you will be released from the bondage of Karma.
explanation: Even the wise men are confused about Karma (action) and Akarma (inaction). I shall now explain to you the truth about Karma. Knowing the truth, O Arjuna, you will be released from the bondage of Karma.

sanskrit: कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मणः । अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गतिः ॥ ४.१७ ॥
hindi: कर्मका स्वरूप भी जानना चाहिये और अकर्मका स्वरूप भी जानना चाहिये तथा विकर्मका स्वरूप भी जानना चाहिये; क्योंकि कर्मकी गति गहन है|
english: One should know the principle of Karma (action) Akarma (inaction) and forbidden Karma, (forbidden action). The philosophy behind Karma is very deep and mysterious. So listen closely.
explanation: One should know the principle of Karma (action) Akarma (inaction) and forbidden Karma, (forbidden action). The philosophy behind Karma is very deep and mysterious. So listen closely.

sanskrit: कर्मण्यकर्म यः पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म यः । स बुद्धिमान्मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्मकृत् ॥ ४.१८ ॥
hindi: जो मनुष्य कर्ममें अकर्म देखता है और जो अकर्ममें कर्म देखता है, वह मनुष्योंमें बुद्धिमान् है और वह योगी समस्त कर्मोंको करनेवाला है|
english: One who sees action (Karma) in inaction (Akarma), and inaction in action, is a wise man and a great sage. That man who has accomplished all actions is a Yogi.
explanation: One who sees action (Karma) in inaction (Akarma), and inaction in action, is a wise man and a great sage. That man who has accomplished all actions is a Yogi.

sanskrit: यस्य सर्वे समारम्भाः कामसंकल्पवर्जिताः । ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः ॥ ४.१९ ॥
hindi: जिसके सम्पूर्ण शास्त्रसम्मत कर्म बिना कामना और संकल्पके होते हैं तथा जिसके समस्त कर्म ज्ञानरूप अग्निके द्वारा भस्म हो गये हैं, उस महापुरुषको ज्ञानीजन भी पण्डित कहते हैं|
english: One who does all Karma without desire or attachment for the fruits of his Karma and one whose actions are burnt up in the of Gyan or wisdom, is regarded by even the wise men as a sage.
explanation: One who does all Karma without desire or attachment for the fruits of his Karma and one whose actions are burnt up in the of Gyan or wisdom, is regarded by even the wise men as a sage.

sanskrit: निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः । शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥ ४.२१ ॥
hindi: जिसका अन्तःकरण और इन्द्रियोंके सहित शरीर जीता हुआ है और जिसने समस्त भोगोंकी सामग्रीका परित्याग कर दिया है, ऐसा आशारहित पुरुष केवल शरीर-सम्बन्धी कर्म करता हुआ भी पापोंको नहीं प्राप्त होता|
english: That person, O Arjuna, who has conquered his body and mind, who has given up all enjoyments and pleasures of the world, and who performs action only for the sake of maintaining his body, does not encounter or subiect himself to sin.
explanation: That person, O Arjuna, who has conquered his body and mind, who has given up all enjoyments and pleasures of the world, and who performs action only for the sake of maintaining his body, does not encounter or subiect himself to sin.

sanskrit: यदृच्छालाभसंतुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सरः । समः सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते ॥ ४.२२ ॥
hindi: जो बिना इच्छाके अपने-आप प्राप्त हुए पदार्थमें सदा सन्तुष्ट रहता है, जिसमें ईर्ष्याका सर्वथा अभाव हो गया है, जो हर्ष-शोक आदि द्वन्द्वों से सर्वथा अतीत हो गया है- ऐसा सिद्धि और असिद्धिमें सम रहनेवाला कर्मयोगी कर्म करता हुआ भी उनसे नहीं बँधता|
english: A person who is content with what he has who has no feeling of desire for other things, or uncertainty of any type, who has shed his envious feelings and who is above all even-minded in success and failure, such a person is no longer bound by Karma even if he still performs Karma.
explanation: A person who is content with what he has who has no feeling of desire for other things, or uncertainty of any type, who has shed his envious feelings and who is above all even-minded in success and failure, such a person is no longer bound by Karma even if he still performs Karma.

sanskrit: गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः । यज्ञायाचरतः कर्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते ॥ ४.२३ ॥
hindi: जिसकी आसक्ति सर्वथा नष्ट हो गयी है, जो देहाभिमान और ममतासे रहित हो गया है, जिसका चित्त निरन्तर परमात्माके ज्ञानमें स्थित रहता है – ऐसा केवल यज्ञसम्पादनके लिये कर्म करनेवाले मनुष्यके सम्पूर्ण कर्म भलीभाँति विलीन हो जाते हैं|
english: Lord Krishna continued: A person who has no attachment to anything whatsoever in this world, whose heart is set on acquiring Gyan (wisdom), who works for the sake of sacrifice, and is a totally liberated person from the world, all actions for this person melt away and are meaningless.
explanation: Lord Krishna continued: A person who has no attachment to anything whatsoever in this world, whose heart is set on acquiring Gyan (wisdom), who works for the sake of sacrifice, and is a totally liberated person from the world, all actions for this person melt away and are meaningless.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम् । ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना ॥ ४.२...
hindi: जिस यज्ञमें अर्पण अर्थात् स्रुवा आदि भी ब्रह्म है और हवन किये जानेयोग्य द्रव्य भी ब्रह्म है तथा ब्रह्मरूप कर्ताके द्वारा ब्रह्मरूप अग्निमें आहुति देनारूप क्रिया भी ब्रह्म है – उस ब्रह्मकर्ममें स्थित – रहनेवाले योगीद्वारा प्राप्त किये जानेयोग्य फल भी ब्रह्म ही है|
english: The offerings in a sacrifice are Brahma (God), the sacrificial fire is Brahma (God), the one who performs the sacrifice is Brahma, the sacrifice itself is Brahma, the person performing the action of sacrifice is placed in Brahma and will reach Brahma, the ultimate goal of sacrifice.
explanation: The offerings in a sacrifice are Brahma (God), the sacrificial fire is Brahma (God), the one who performs the sacrifice is Brahma, the sacrifice itself is Brahma, the person performing the action of sacrifice is placed in Brahma and will reach Brahma, the ultimate goal of sacrifice.

sanskrit: दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्युपासते । ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति ॥ ४.२५ ॥
hindi: दूसरे योगीजन देवताओंके पूजनरूप यज्ञका ही भलीभाँति अनुष्ठान किया करते हैं और अन्य योगीजन परब्रह्म परमात्मारूप अग्निमें अभेददर्शनरूप यज्ञके द्वारा ही आत्मरूप यज्ञका हवन किया करते हैं|
english: The Lord continued: Some Yogis prefer to perform sacrifice through the worship of deities (gods and god esses): other Yogis perform sacrifice to Brahma in the form of fire.
explanation: The Lord continued: Some Yogis prefer to perform sacrifice through the worship of deities (gods and god esses): other Yogis perform sacrifice to Brahma in the form of fire.

sanskrit: श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति । शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति ॥ ४.२६...
hindi: अन्य योगीजन श्रोत्र आदि समस्त इन्द्रियोंको संयमरूप अग्नियोंमें हवन किया करते हैं और दूसरे योगीलोग शब्दादि समस्त विषयोंको इन्द्रियरूप अग्नियोंमें हवन किया करते हैं|
english: Some offer, in the fire of self-control, one of their senses such as their hearing. (This signifies that the signifies has no attachment to any of his senses.)Others offer sensual objects (objects of perception), such as sound, in the fire of controlled senses.Signifying that the sacrificer has the will-power and restraint not to let his senses be influenced by evil, sin, or sensual objects of destruction.
explanation: Some offer, in the fire of self-control, one of their senses such as their hearing. (This signifies that the signifies has no attachment to any of his senses.)Others offer sensual objects (objects of perception), such as sound, in the fire of controlled senses.Signifying that the sacrificer has the will-power and restraint not to let his senses be influenced by evil, sin, or sensual objects of destruction.

sanskrit: सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे । आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते ॥ ४.२७ ॥
hindi: दूसरे योगीजन इन्द्रियोंकी सम्पूर्ण क्रियाओंको और प्राणों की समस्त क्रियाओंको ज्ञानसे प्रकाशित आत्मसंयमयोगरूप अग्निमें हवन किया करते हैं|
english: Some Yogis sacrifice all the functions of their senses ans all their vital functions of life, into the fire of Yoga, in the shape of self control, which is kindled by Gyan (wisdom). (This signifies the sacrificer’s one-mindedness with the Lord, the Supreme Goal).
explanation: Some Yogis sacrifice all the functions of their senses ans all their vital functions of life, into the fire of Yoga, in the shape of self control, which is kindled by Gyan (wisdom). (This signifies the sacrificer’s one-mindedness with the Lord, the Supreme Goal).

sanskrit: द्रव्ययज्ञास्तपोयज्ञा योगयज्ञास्तथापरे । स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाश्च यतयः संशितव्रताः ॥ ४.२८ ॥
hindi: कई पुरुष द्रव्यसम्बन्धी यज्ञ करनेवाले हैं, कितने ही तपस्यारूप यज्ञ करनेवाले हैं तथा दूसरे कितने ही योगरूप यज्ञ करनेवाले हैं, कितने ही अहिंसादि तीक्ष्ण व्रतोंसे युक्त यत्नशील पुरुष स्वाध्यायरूप ज्ञानयज्ञ करनेवाले हैं|
english: Sacrifices come in many forms. Giving away material wealth for charity is a sacrifice. Austerity is a sacrifice. Yoga is a sacrifice. Making vows and promises involve sacrifice. Even self-study is a sacrifice.
explanation: Sacrifices come in many forms. Giving away material wealth for charity is a sacrifice. Austerity is a sacrifice. Yoga is a sacrifice. Making vows and promises involve sacrifice. Even self-study is a sacrifice.

sanskrit: अपाने जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणेऽपानं तथापरे प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः अपरे नियताहाराः प्र...
hindi: दूसरे कितने ही योगीजन अपानवायुमें प्राणवायुको हवन करते हैं, वैसे ही अन्य योगीजन प्राणवायुमें अपानवायुको हवन करते हैं तथा अन्य कितने ही नियमित आहार करनेवाले प्राणायामपरायण पुरुष प्राण और अपानकी गतिको रोककर प्राणोंको प्राणोंमें ही हवन किया करते हैं। ये सभी साधक यज्ञोंद्वारा पापोंका नाश कर देनेवाले और यज्ञोंको जाननेवाले हैं|
english: Other Yogis (wise men) perform sacrifice by controlling the amount of breaths they take. By holding in their breath for a long periond of time whilepronouncing My name, they increase their life’s longevity. This act is known as PRANAYAM.Others perform sacrifice by controlling what they eat,(fasting). Those whose sins have been destroyed by sacrifice, understand the power and importance of sacrifice.
explanation: Other Yogis (wise men) perform sacrifice by controlling the amount of breaths they take. By holding in their breath for a long periond of time whilepronouncing My name, they increase their life’s longevity. This act is known as PRANAYAM.Others perform sacrifice by controlling what they eat,(fasting). Those whose sins have been destroyed by sacrifice, understand the power and importance of sacrifice.

sanskrit: नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतोऽन्यः कुरुसत्तम ॥ ४.३१ ॥
hindi: हे कुरुश्रेष्ठ अर्जुन! यज्ञसे बचे हुए अमृतका अनुभव करनेवाले योगीजन सनातन परब्रह्म परमात्माको प्राप्त होते हैं। और यज्ञ न करनेवाले पुरुषके लिये तो यह मनुष्यलोक भी सुखदायक नहीं है, फिर परलोक है कैसे सुखदायक हो सकता है ?
english: O Arjuna, only those people who have sacrificed to achieve wisdom and knowledge of Gyan, go to Brahma, (the creator of all beings and God of Wisdom in the World). Without performing some sort of sacrifice in life, one cannot possibly remain happy in this world, not to mention the afterworl
explanation: O Arjuna, only those people who have sacrificed to achieve wisdom and knowledge of Gyan, go to Brahma, (the creator of all beings and God of Wisdom in the World). Without performing some sort of sacrifice in life, one cannot possibly remain happy in this world, not to mention the afterworl

sanskrit: एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे । कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे ॥ ४.३२ ॥
hindi: इसी प्रकार और भी बहुत तरहके यज्ञ वेदकी वाणीमें विस्तारसे कहे गये हैं। उन सबको तू मन, इन्द्रिय और शरीरकी क्रियाद्वारा सम्पन्न होनेवाले जान, इस प्रकार तत्त्वसे जानकर उनके अनुष्ठानद्वारा तू कर्मबन्धनसे सर्वथा मुक्त हो जायगा|
english: The Lord continued:O Arjuna, many sacrifices are mentioned extensively throughout the Vedas. All of these sacrifices involve some sort of action or Karma to be performed. With this knowledge in mind, you will achieve the state of unattached Karma, or action done without the expectation of any results.
explanation: The Lord continued:O Arjuna, many sacrifices are mentioned extensively throughout the Vedas. All of these sacrifices involve some sort of action or Karma to be performed. With this knowledge in mind, you will achieve the state of unattached Karma, or action done without the expectation of any results.

sanskrit: श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप । सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते ॥ ४.३३ ॥
hindi: हे परंतप अर्जुन ! द्रव्यमय यज्ञकी अपेक्षा ज्ञानयज्ञ अत्यन्त श्रेष्ठ है, तथा यावन्मात्र सम्पूर्ण कर्म ज्ञानमें समाप्त हो जाते हैं|
english: O Arjuna, sacrifice of wisdom (Gyan) is always better than sacrifice of material objects because all actions end with Gyan.
explanation: O Arjuna, sacrifice of wisdom (Gyan) is always better than sacrifice of material objects because all actions end with Gyan.

sanskrit: तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया । उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ॥ ४.३४ ॥
hindi: उस ज्ञानको तू तत्त्वदर्शी ज्ञानियोंके पास जाकर समझ, उनको भलीभाँति दण्डवत् प्रणाम करनेसे, उनकी सेवा करनेसे और कपट छोड़कर सरलतापूर्वक प्रश्न करनेसे वे परमात्मतत्त्वको भलीभाँति जाननेवाले ज्ञानी महात्मा तुझे उस तत्त्वज्ञानका उपदेश करेंगे|
english: The Lord advised:O Arjuna, learn the principles of Gyan and ask questions about Gyan respectfully and with determination, from those who fully understand the principles of Gyan. Thay will explain “Gyan” to you. Knowing Gyan, you will never be in the confused state as you are in now.
explanation: The Lord advised:O Arjuna, learn the principles of Gyan and ask questions about Gyan respectfully and with determination, from those who fully understand the principles of Gyan. Thay will explain “Gyan” to you. Knowing Gyan, you will never be in the confused state as you are in now.

sanskrit: यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव । येन भूतान्यशेषेण द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि ॥ ४.३५ ॥
hindi: जिसको जानकर फिर तू इस प्रकार मोहको नहीं प्राप्त होगा तथा हे अर्जुन ! जिस ज्ञानके द्वारा तू सम्पूर्ण भूतोंको नि:शेषभावसे पहले अपनेमें’ और पीछे मुझ सच्चिदानन्दघन परमात्मामें देखेगा|
english: The Lord continued:O Arjuna, through Gyan, you will see all beings within yourself, and thereafter, all beings in Me.
explanation: The Lord continued:O Arjuna, through Gyan, you will see all beings within yourself, and thereafter, all beings in Me.

sanskrit: अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः । सर्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि ॥ ४.३६ ॥
hindi: यदि तू अन्य सब पापियोंसे भी अधिक पाप करनेवाला है; तो भी तू ज्ञानरूप नौकाद्वारा नि:संदेह तू सम्पूर्ण पाप-समुद्रसे भलीभाँति तर जायगा|
english: O Arjuna, even if you are the greatest sinner in the world, this river of sins can be crossed with the boat of Gyan.
explanation: O Arjuna, even if you are the greatest sinner in the world, this river of sins can be crossed with the boat of Gyan.

sanskrit: यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन । ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा ॥ ४.३७ ॥
hindi: क्योंकि हे अर्जुन ! जैसे प्रज्वलित अग्नि ईंधनोंको भस्ममय कर देता है, वैसे ही ज्ञानरूप अग्नि सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंको भस्ममय कर देता है|
english: O Arjuna, as the burnig fire reduces fuel to ashes, in this same way, Karma (attached Karma binding a person to the material world), is burnt down with the fire of Gyan.
explanation: O Arjuna, as the burnig fire reduces fuel to ashes, in this same way, Karma (attached Karma binding a person to the material world), is burnt down with the fire of Gyan.

sanskrit: न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते । तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति ॥ ४.३८ ॥
hindi: इस संसारमें ज्ञानके समान पवित्र करनेवाला निःसंदेह कुछ भी नहीं है । उस ज्ञानको कितने ही कालसे कर्मयोगके द्वारा शुद्धान्तःकरण हुआ मनुष्य अपने आप ही आत्मामें पा लेता है|
english: In this world, O Arjuna, there is no greater purifier than Gyan or wisdom itself. The person who has mastered Yoga to perfection feels wisdom in his soul at the proper time.
explanation: In this world, O Arjuna, there is no greater purifier than Gyan or wisdom itself. The person who has mastered Yoga to perfection feels wisdom in his soul at the proper time.

sanskrit: श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः । ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥ ४.३९ ॥
hindi: जितेन्द्रिय, साधनपरायण और श्रद्धावान् मनुष्य ज्ञानको प्राप्त होता है तथा ज्ञानको प्राप्त होकर वह बिना विलम्बके – तत्काल ही भगवत्प्राप्तिरूप परम – शान्तिको प्राप्त हो जाता है|
english: To abtain Gyan, one must conquer the senses, and develop a real devotion and faith towards the Lord. When one obtains Gyan, he has discovered the key to Supreme peace.
explanation: To abtain Gyan, one must conquer the senses, and develop a real devotion and faith towards the Lord. When one obtains Gyan, he has discovered the key to Supreme peace.

sanskrit: योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसंछिन्नसंशयम् । आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनंजय ॥ ४.४१ ॥
hindi: हे धनञ्जय ! जिसने कर्मयोगकी विधिसे समस्त कर्मोंका परमात्मामें अर्पण कर दिया है और जिसने विवेकद्वारा समस्त संशयोंका नाश कर दिया है, ऐसे वशमें किये हुए अन्तःकरणवाले पुरुषको कर्म नहीं बाँधते|
english: One who has rid himself of attached Karma by practising Yoga and one who has rid himself of doubts by achieving Gyan, he is a self-realized person and is not bound by attached Karma.
explanation: One who has rid himself of attached Karma by practising Yoga and one who has rid himself of doubts by achieving Gyan, he is a self-realized person and is not bound by attached Karma.

sanskrit: तस्मादज्ञानसम्भूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनात्मनः । छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत ॥ ४.४२ ॥
hindi: इसलिये हे भरतवंशी अर्जुन ! तू हृदयमें स्थित इस अज्ञानजनित अपने संशयका विवेकज्ञानरूप तलवारद्वारा छेदन करके समत्वरूप कर्मयोगमें स्थित हो जा और युद्धके लिये खड़ा हो जा|
english: Hence, be established in yoga by cutting the ignorance born doubt about the self with the sword of knowledge. Stand up, O Bharata.
explanation: Hence, be established in yoga by cutting the ignorance born doubt about the self with the sword of knowledge. Stand up, O Bharata.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच । संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम् ...
hindi: अर्जुन बोले– हे कृष्ण ! आप कर्मोंके संन्यासकी और फिर कर्मयोगकी प्रशंसा करते हैं । इसलिये इन दोनोंमेंसे जो एक मेरे लिये भलीभाँति निश्चित कल्याणकारक साधन हो, उसको कहिये|
english: Arjuna asked the Great Lord: Dear Krishna, I am still confused. I have heard praise of Yoga as well as of Sannyaas (Holy Pilgrimage), from You. Which one of these two O Lord, is the best path for me?
explanation: Arjuna asked the Great Lord: Dear Krishna, I am still confused. I have heard praise of Yoga as well as of Sannyaas (Holy Pilgrimage), from You. Which one of these two O Lord, is the best path for me?

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥ ५....
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले- कर्मसंन्यास और कर्मयोग— ये दोनों ही परम कल्याणके करनेवाले हैं, परन्तु उन दोनोंमें भी कर्मसंन्याससे कर्मयोग साधनमें सुगम होनेसे श्रेष्ठ है|
english: Oh Arjuna, both the paths of Yoga and Sannyaas lead one to supreme bliss and happiness. However, I shall always regard Yoga as the better path to the attainment of supreme peace and unity with Me, than the path of Sannyaas.
explanation: Oh Arjuna, both the paths of Yoga and Sannyaas lead one to supreme bliss and happiness. However, I shall always regard Yoga as the better path to the attainment of supreme peace and unity with Me, than the path of Sannyaas.

sanskrit: ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति । निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ...
hindi: हे अर्जुन! जो पुरुष न किसीसे द्वेष करता है और न किसीकी आकांक्षा करता है, वह कर्मयोगी सदा संन्यासी ही समझने योग्य है; क्योंकि राग द्वेषादि द्वन्द्वोंसे रहित पुरुष सुखपूर्वक संसारबन्धनसे मुक्त हो जाता है|
english: Oh Arjuna a true Sannyaasi is one who has no doubts about anything he does or encounters in his life; who has no enemies whatsoever, and above all, who is free from all desires (for material objects). If he has accomplished all these bondages of the world.
explanation: Oh Arjuna a true Sannyaasi is one who has no doubts about anything he does or encounters in his life; who has no enemies whatsoever, and above all, who is free from all desires (for material objects). If he has accomplished all these bondages of the world.

sanskrit: सांख्ययोगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः । एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् ॥ ५.४ ॥
hindi: उपर्युक्त संन्यास और कर्मयोगको मूर्खलोग पृथक् पृथक् फल देनेवाले कहते हैं न कि पण्डितजन, क्योंकि दोनोंमेंसे एकमें भी सम्यक् प्रकारसे स्थित पुरुष दोनोंके फलरूप परमात्माको प्राप्त होता है|
english: Oh Arjuna, in essense, Yoga and Sannyaas are the same because both of these achieve the same goal. Those who consider Yoga and Sannyaas as two different paths leading to the achievement of two different goals, are ignorant.When one is fully absorbed and established in either one of Yoga or Sannyaas, he achieves the results of both of these sacred institutions.
explanation: Oh Arjuna, in essense, Yoga and Sannyaas are the same because both of these achieve the same goal. Those who consider Yoga and Sannyaas as two different paths leading to the achievement of two different goals, are ignorant.When one is fully absorbed and established in either one of Yoga or Sannyaas, he achieves the results of both of these sacred institutions.

sanskrit: यत्सांख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते । एकं सांख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स: पश्यति ॥ ५.५ ॥
hindi: ज्ञानयोगियों द्वारा जो परमधाम प्राप्त किया जाता है, कर्मयोगियों द्वारा भी वही प्राप्त किया जाता है। इसलिये जो पुरुष ज्ञानयोग और कर्मयोगको फलरूपमें एक देखता है, वही यथार्थ देखता है|
english: Yoga or Sannyaas (together known as Sankyoga) both attain the same goal for he who follows either of these paths. Only he who considers Yoga and Sannyaas as one, sees the ultimate Truth (the Lord).
explanation: Yoga or Sannyaas (together known as Sankyoga) both attain the same goal for he who follows either of these paths. Only he who considers Yoga and Sannyaas as one, sees the ultimate Truth (the Lord).

sanskrit: संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः । योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥ ५.६ ॥
hindi: परन्तु हे अर्जुन ! कर्मयोगके बिना संन्यास अर्थात् मन, इन्द्रिय और शरीरद्वारा होनेवाले सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंमें कर्तापनका त्याग प्राप्त होना कठिन है और भगवत्स्वरूपको मनन करनेवाला कर्मयोगी परब्रह्म परमात्माको शीघ्र ही प्राप्त हो जाता है|
english: The Blessed Lord spoke in His Divine voice:O my dear Arjuna, in order for one to achieve Sannyaas, he must practise Karmyoga (doing Karma or actions without attachment or desire to see the results of those actions). If one is successful in performing Karmyoga (that is Karma without attachment to results), he shall realize God very quickly.
explanation: The Blessed Lord spoke in His Divine voice:O my dear Arjuna, in order for one to achieve Sannyaas, he must practise Karmyoga (doing Karma or actions without attachment or desire to see the results of those actions). If one is successful in performing Karmyoga (that is Karma without attachment to results), he shall realize God very quickly.

sanskrit: योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः । सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥ ५.७ ॥
hindi: जिसका मन अपने वशमें है, जो जितेन्द्रिय एवं विशुद्ध अन्तःकरणवाला है और सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंका आत्मरूप परमात्मा ही जिसका आत्मा है, ऐसा कर्मयोगी कर्म करता हुआ भी लिप्त नहीं होता|
english: The Lord spoke onwards:The Karmyogi is a purified being who has conquered the self. He does not centre his life around himeself but around the achievement of being one with God.He detaches himeself from his senses, centering all his thoughts on God and achieving everlasting peace and happiness by breaking free of all material bondage to the world.
explanation: The Lord spoke onwards:The Karmyogi is a purified being who has conquered the self. He does not centre his life around himeself but around the achievement of being one with God.He detaches himeself from his senses, centering all his thoughts on God and achieving everlasting peace and happiness by breaking free of all material bondage to the world.

sanskrit: नैव किंचित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् । पश्यञ्श्रृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्\u200cगच्छन्स्वपञ्...
hindi: तत्त्वको जाननेवाला सांख्ययोगी तो देखता हुआ, सुनता हुआ, स्पर्श करता हुआ, सूँघता हुआ, भोजन करता हुआ, गमन करता हुआ, सोता हुआ, श्वास लेता हुआ, बोलता हुआ, त्यागता हुआ, ग्रहण करता हुआ तथा आँखोंको खोलता और मूँदता हुआ भी, सब इन्द्रियाँ अपने-अपने अर्थोंमें बरत रही हैं – इस प्रकार समझकर निःसन्देह ऐसा माने कि मैं कुछ भी नहीं करता हूँ|
english: He who comes to the realization that, although he is physically performing actions such as seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, breathing, speaking, receiving, sacrificing, opening and closing eyes, and so on, that he is actually doing nothing, is a Yogi. O Arjuna, the truly wise Yogi knows that in reality, only the senses are acting among their sense objects.
explanation: He who comes to the realization that, although he is physically performing actions such as seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, breathing, speaking, receiving, sacrificing, opening and closing eyes, and so on, that he is actually doing nothing, is a Yogi. O Arjuna, the truly wise Yogi knows that in reality, only the senses are acting among their sense objects.

sanskrit: प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् ॥ ५.९ ॥
hindi: तत्त्वको जाननेवाला सांख्ययोगी तो देखता हुआ, सुनता हुआ, स्पर्श करता हुआ, सूँघता हुआ, भोजन करता हुआ, गमन करता हुआ, सोता हुआ, श्वास लेता हुआ, बोलता हुआ, त्यागता हुआ, ग्रहण करता हुआ तथा आँखोंको खोलता और मूँदता हुआ भी, सब इन्द्रियाँ अपने-अपने अर्थोंमें बरत रही हैं – इस प्रकार समझकर निःसन्देह ऐसा माने कि मैं कुछ भी नहीं करता हूँ|
english: He who comes to the realization that, although he is physically performing actions such as seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, breathing, speaking, receiving, sacrificing, opening and closing eyes, and so on, that he is actually doing nothing, is a Yogi. O Arjuna, the truly wise Yogi knows that in reality, only the senses are acting among their sense objects.
explanation: He who comes to the realization that, although he is physically performing actions such as seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, breathing, speaking, receiving, sacrificing, opening and closing eyes, and so on, that he is actually doing nothing, is a Yogi. O Arjuna, the truly wise Yogi knows that in reality, only the senses are acting among their sense objects.

sanskrit: कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि । योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्मशुद्धये ॥ ५.११ ॥
hindi: कर्मयोगी ममत्वबुद्धिरहित केवल इन्द्रिय, मन, बुद्धि और शरीरद्वारा भी आसक्तिको त्यागकर अन्तःकरणकी शुद्धिके लिये कर्म करते हैं|
english: O Arjuna, Yogis purify their souls by abolishing any feelings of attachment within themselves by performing Karma through mind, body, intellect, and senses.
explanation: O Arjuna, Yogis purify their souls by abolishing any feelings of attachment within themselves by performing Karma through mind, body, intellect, and senses.

sanskrit: युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् । अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥ ५.१२ ॥
hindi: कर्मयोगी कर्मोंके कर्मोंके फलका त्याग करके भगवत्प्राप्तिरूप शान्तिको प्राप्त होता है और सकाम पुरुष कामनाकी प्रेरणासे फलमें आसक्त होकर बँधता है|
english: When a Yogi has reached a state of true self-realization by leaving all fruits and results of Karma in the hands of the Lord, he achieves true peace and happiness.However, he who does Karma (actions) for a selfish motive and for the attainment of fruits of his actions, never becomes free of the bondages of desire for material pleasures.
explanation: When a Yogi has reached a state of true self-realization by leaving all fruits and results of Karma in the hands of the Lord, he achieves true peace and happiness.However, he who does Karma (actions) for a selfish motive and for the attainment of fruits of his actions, never becomes free of the bondages of desire for material pleasures.

sanskrit: सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी । नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् ॥ ५.१३ ॥
hindi: अन्तःकरण जिसके वशमें है ऐसा सांख्ययोगका आचरण करनेवाला पुरुष न करता हुआ और न करवाता हुआ ही नवद्वारोंवाले शरीररूप घरमें सब कर्मोंको मनसे त्यागकर आनन्दपूर्वक सच्चिदानन्दघन परमात्माके स्वरूपमें स्थित रहता है|
english: A person, having self-control, and mentally freeing himself of all actions (by the uninterest in results of actions), in really performs no actions whatsoever. By mentally detaching himself of all Karma, a man may live in true bliss and peace in his body of nine openings.
explanation: A person, having self-control, and mentally freeing himself of all actions (by the uninterest in results of actions), in really performs no actions whatsoever. By mentally detaching himself of all Karma, a man may live in true bliss and peace in his body of nine openings.

sanskrit: न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः । न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते ॥ ५.१४ ॥
hindi: परमेश्वर मनुष्योंके न तो कर्तापनकी, न कर्मोंकी और न कर्मफलके संयोगकी ही रचना करते हैं; किन्तु स्वभाव ही बर्त रहा है|
english: The Lord does not create the performance of actions (or Karma ), nor Karma itself, nor even the result of Karma. It is simply all due to nature performing its function in the world.(However, nature derives its motivation power from God.)
explanation: The Lord does not create the performance of actions (or Karma ), nor Karma itself, nor even the result of Karma. It is simply all due to nature performing its function in the world.(However, nature derives its motivation power from God.)

sanskrit: नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः । अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः ॥ ५.१५ ॥
hindi: सर्वव्यापी परमेश्वर भी न किसीके पापकर्मको और न किसीके शुभकर्मको ही ग्रहण करता है; किन्तु अज्ञानके द्वारा ज्ञान ढका हुआ है, उससे सब अज्ञानी मनुष्य मोहित हो रहे हैं|
english: I, the all pervading Lord receives neither the good nor the bad Karma of anyone. People are from time to time deluded because their Knowledge (Gyan) is Covered up or blinded by ignorance (Agyan).
explanation: I, the all pervading Lord receives neither the good nor the bad Karma of anyone. People are from time to time deluded because their Knowledge (Gyan) is Covered up or blinded by ignorance (Agyan).

sanskrit: ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः । तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥ ५.१६ ॥
hindi: परन्तु जिनका वह अज्ञान परमात्माके तत्त्वज्ञानद्वारा नष्ट कर दिया गया है, उनका वह ज्ञान सूर्यके सदृश उस सच्चिदानन्दघन परमात्माको प्रकाशित कर देता है|
english: The Lord continued: People’s achievement of knowledge of their SELF has allowed them to destroy their ignorance or remove their lack of knowledge (Agyan). They soon find that the knowledge they acquire radiates the light of God within them.
explanation: The Lord continued: People’s achievement of knowledge of their SELF has allowed them to destroy their ignorance or remove their lack of knowledge (Agyan). They soon find that the knowledge they acquire radiates the light of God within them.

sanskrit: तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणा: | गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषा:॥ ५.१७...
hindi: जिनका मन तद्रूप हो रहा है, जिनकी बुद्धि तद्रूप हो रही है और सच्चिदानन्दघन परमात्मामें ही जिनकी निरन्तर एकीभावसे स्थिति है, ऐसे तत्परायण पुरुष ज्ञानके द्वारा पापरहित होकर अपुनरावृत्तिको अर्थात् परमगतिको प्राप्त होते हैं|
english: The Lord spoke: O Arjuna, total liberation and everlasting peace is achieved only by those whose mind and intellect constantly dwells on God and who have destroyed all sin by achieving Gyan (Knowledge)
explanation: The Lord spoke: O Arjuna, total liberation and everlasting peace is achieved only by those whose mind and intellect constantly dwells on God and who have destroyed all sin by achieving Gyan (Knowledge)

sanskrit: विद्याविनयसंपन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि । शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः ॥ ५.१८ ॥
hindi: वे ज्ञानीजन विद्या और विनययुक्त ब्राह्मणमें तथा गौ, हाथी, कुत्ते और चाण्डालमें भी समदर्शी ही होते हैं|
english: The wise men treat everybody as equals O Arjuna, whether it be a learned and cultured Brahman, a cow, an elephant, or a dog and an outcaste. He not differentiate between anybody.
explanation: The wise men treat everybody as equals O Arjuna, whether it be a learned and cultured Brahman, a cow, an elephant, or a dog and an outcaste. He not differentiate between anybody.

sanskrit: इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः । निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद्ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः ॥ ५...
hindi: जिनका मन समभावमें स्थित है, उनके द्वारा इस जीवित अवस्थामें ही सम्पूर्ण संसार जीत लिया गया है, क्योंकि सच्चिदानन्दघन परमात्मा निर्दोष और सम है, इससे वे सच्चिदानन्दघन परमात्मामें ही स्थित हैं|
english: O Arjuna, those who are even-minded and treat everybody as equals in all respects, have reached the ultimate goal of life and have conquered the whole world because God is perfect, without defect, and makes no distinction also. Therefore, they are,in reality, one with God.
explanation: O Arjuna, those who are even-minded and treat everybody as equals in all respects, have reached the ultimate goal of life and have conquered the whole world because God is perfect, without defect, and makes no distinction also. Therefore, they are,in reality, one with God.

sanskrit: बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत् सुखम् । स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते ॥ ५.२१ ॥
hindi: बाहरके विषयों में आसक्तिरहित अन्तःकरणवाला साधक आत्मामें स्थित जो ध्यानजनित सात्त्विक आनन्द है, उसको प्राप्त होता है; तदनन्तर वह सच्चिदानन्दघन परब्रह्म परमात्माके ध्यानरूप योगमें अभिन्नभावसे स्थित पुरुष अक्षय आनन्दका अनुभव करता है|
english: O Arjuna, a person who has totally detached himself from the material world and is happy and satisfied within his own self, attains eternal peace. When a person is truly established in the Yoga of God (service, devotion and prayers to Him), he experiences true, eternal bliss without a doubt, O Arjuna.
explanation: O Arjuna, a person who has totally detached himself from the material world and is happy and satisfied within his own self, attains eternal peace. When a person is truly established in the Yoga of God (service, devotion and prayers to Him), he experiences true, eternal bliss without a doubt, O Arjuna.

sanskrit: ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते । आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः ॥ ५.२२ ॥
hindi: जो ये इन्द्रिय तथा विषयोंके संयोगसे उत्पन्न होनेवाले सब भोग हैं, यद्यपि विषयी पुरुषोंको सुखरूप भासते हैं तो भी दुःखके ही हेतु हैं और आदि अन्तवाले अर्थात् अनित्य हैं। इसलिये हे अर्जुन ! । बुद्धिमान् विवेकी पुरुष उनमें नहीं रमता|
english: When one’s senses make contact with sensual, material goods, Arjuna, these desire-evoking objects are the cause of misery (these objects may appear enjoyable to material pleasure-seekers).These enjoyments are temporary, having a beginning and an end. When one develops a habitual desire for these artificial enjoyments. O Arjuna, he falls into deep misery and irrational behaviour as soon as these pleasures have disappeared from him.
explanation: When one’s senses make contact with sensual, material goods, Arjuna, these desire-evoking objects are the cause of misery (these objects may appear enjoyable to material pleasure-seekers).These enjoyments are temporary, having a beginning and an end. When one develops a habitual desire for these artificial enjoyments. O Arjuna, he falls into deep misery and irrational behaviour as soon as these pleasures have disappeared from him.

sanskrit: शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् । कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः ॥ ५.२३ ॥
hindi: जो पुरुष अन्तरात्मामें ही सुखवाला है, आत्मामें ही रमण करनेवाला है तथा जो आत्मामें ही ज्ञानवाला है, वह सच्चिदानन्दघन परब्रह्म परमात्माके साथ एकीभावको प्राप्त सांख्ययोगी शान्त ब्रह्मको प्राप्त होता है|
english: One who is truly happy, peaceful and enlightened with his Soul, that wise man (Yogi) has combined with the supreme Soul (God) as one and attains freedom from all bondages to the world.
explanation: One who is truly happy, peaceful and enlightened with his Soul, that wise man (Yogi) has combined with the supreme Soul (God) as one and attains freedom from all bondages to the world.

sanskrit: योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः । स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥ ५.२४ ॥
hindi: जिनके सब पाप नष्ट हो गये हैं, जिनके सब संशय ज्ञानके द्वारा निवृत्त हो गये हैं, जो सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके हितमें रत हैं और जिनका जीता हुआ मन निश्चलभावसे परमात्मामें स्थित है, वे ब्रह्मवेत्ता पुरुष शान्त ब्रह्मको प्राप्त होते हैं|
english: One who is truly happy, peaceful and enlightened with his Soul, that wise man (Yogi) has combined with the supreme Soul (God) as one and attains freedom from all bondages to the world.
explanation: One who is truly happy, peaceful and enlightened with his Soul, that wise man (Yogi) has combined with the supreme Soul (God) as one and attains freedom from all bondages to the world.

sanskrit: लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः । छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥ ५.२५ ॥
hindi: जिनके सब पाप नष्ट हो गये हैं, जिनके सब संशय ज्ञानके द्वारा निवृत्त हो गये हैं, जो सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके हितमें रत हैं और जिनका जीता हुआ मन निश्चलभावसे परमात्मामें स्थित है, वे ब्रह्मवेत्ता पुरुष शान्त ब्रह्मको प्राप्त होते हैं|
english: Those wise men or sages who have abolished and destroyed all their sins with the achievement of true Gyan (Knowledge), and who have devoted themselves to the welfare of other beings, attain supreme and eternal peace.
explanation: Those wise men or sages who have abolished and destroyed all their sins with the achievement of true Gyan (Knowledge), and who have devoted themselves to the welfare of other beings, attain supreme and eternal peace.

sanskrit: कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् । अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥ ५.२६ ॥
hindi: काम-क्रोधसे रहित, जीते हुए चित्तवाले, परब्रह्म परमात्माका साक्षात्कार किये हुए ज्ञानी पुरुषों के लिये सब ओरसे शान्त परब्रह्म परमात्मा ही परिपूर्ण हैं|
english: O Arjuna, all of those wise men (Yogis) who have learned to control their minds, have learned to conquer and abolish all of the desires and anger within them, and have truly realized God, attain freedom and liberation from worldly ties in all respects.
explanation: O Arjuna, all of those wise men (Yogis) who have learned to control their minds, have learned to conquer and abolish all of the desires and anger within them, and have truly realized God, attain freedom and liberation from worldly ties in all respects.

sanskrit: स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः । प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥ ...
hindi: बाहरके विषय-भोगोंको न चिन्तन करता हुआ बाहर ही निकालकर और नेत्रोंकी दृष्टिको भृकुटीके बीचमें स्थित करके तथा नासिकामें विचरनेवाले प्राण और अपानवायुको सम करके, जिसकी इन्द्रियाँ, मन और बुद्धि जीती हुई हैं, ऐसा जो मोक्षपरायण मुनि इच्छा, भय और क्रोधसे रहित हो गया है,वह सदा मुक्त ही है|
english: A person who has totally detached himself from sense objects that give temporary pleasure and enjoyment, he who has learned to control and equalize the number of breaths going outwards and inwards through the nostrils, he who has brought his senses, mind and intellect under full control, and he who is desire less fearless and without anger, is always a free and liberated wise man.
explanation: A person who has totally detached himself from sense objects that give temporary pleasure and enjoyment, he who has learned to control and equalize the number of breaths going outwards and inwards through the nostrils, he who has brought his senses, mind and intellect under full control, and he who is desire less fearless and without anger, is always a free and liberated wise man.

sanskrit: यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः । विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः ॥ ५.२८ ॥
hindi: बाहरके विषय-भोगोंको न चिन्तन करता हुआ बाहर ही निकालकर और नेत्रोंकी दृष्टिको भृकुटीके बीचमें स्थित करके तथा नासिकामें विचरनेवाले प्राण और अपानवायुको सम करके, जिसकी इन्द्रियाँ, मन और बुद्धि जीती हुई हैं, ऐसा जो मोक्षपरायण मुनि इच्छा, भय और क्रोधसे रहित हो गया है,वह सदा मुक्त ही है|
english: A person who has totally detached himself from sense objects that give temporary pleasure and enjoyment, he who has learned to control and equalize the number of breaths going outwards and inwards through the nostrils, he who has brought his senses, mind and intellect under full control, and he who is desire less fearless and without anger, is always a free and liberated wise man.
