english: Arjuna responded in relief: Now that I see your gentle human form Dear Krishna, I have returned back to my normal self with a peaceful mind and heart.
explanation: Arjuna responded in relief: Now that I see your gentle human form Dear Krishna, I have returned back to my normal self with a peaceful mind and heart.

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः ॥ ...
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले ——मेरा जो चतुर्भुज रूप तुमने देखा हैं, यह सुदुर्दर्श है अर्थात् इसके दर्शन बड़े ही दुर्लभ हैं । देवता भी सदा इस रूप के दर्शन की आकांक्षा करते रहते हैं |
english: The Blessed lord said: Dear Arjuna, the Divine form of Myself which you have seen with so much difficulty is an experience which even the other gods and godesses in heaven long to see.
explanation: The Blessed lord said: Dear Arjuna, the Divine form of Myself which you have seen with so much difficulty is an experience which even the other gods and godesses in heaven long to see.

sanskrit: नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया । शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा ॥ ११.५३ ॥
hindi: जिस प्रकार तुमने मुझको देखा है —–इस प्रकार चतुर्भुज रूप वाला मैं न वेदों से, न तप से, न दान से और न यज्ञ से ही देखा जा सकता हूँ ।
english: Not even with such religious acts as ritual offerings, gifts to the unfortunate and poor, and by leading a life only of worship and devotion to Me, can one be so fortunate as to witness what you have.
explanation: Not even with such religious acts as ritual offerings, gifts to the unfortunate and poor, and by leading a life only of worship and devotion to Me, can one be so fortunate as to witness what you have.

sanskrit: भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्य अहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन । ज्ञातुं द्रष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परंतप ॥ ११.५४ ॥
hindi: परन्तु हे परंतप अर्जुन ! अनन्य भक्क्ति के द्वारा इस प्रकार चतुर्भुज रूप वाला मैं प्रत्यक्ष देखने के लिये, तत्व से जानने के लिये तथा प्रवेश करने के लिये अर्थात् एकीभाव से प्राप्त होने के लिये भी शक्य हूँ |
english: It is only by true love and selfless devotion that one can truly come to know Me, see Me in My true form, and be an eternal part of Me.
explanation: It is only by true love and selfless devotion that one can truly come to know Me, see Me in My true form, and be an eternal part of Me.

sanskrit: मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः । निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव ॥ ११.५५ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! जो पुरुष केवल मेरे ही लिये सम्पूर्ण कर्तव्य कर्मों को करने वाला हैं, मेरे परायण है, मेरा भक्त्त हैं, आसक्तिरहित है और सम्पूर्ण भूत प्राणियों में वैर भाव से रहित है वह अनन्य भक्ति युक्त पुरुष मुझको ही प्राप्त होता हैं ।
english: He who truly loves Me; devotes his whole life to performing actions to please only Myself; who recognizes that I am the Supreme and final goal of life; who is free from all attachment and has love for all that I have created; that man comes to truly know who I am.
explanation: He who truly loves Me; devotes his whole life to performing actions to please only Myself; who recognizes that I am the Supreme and final goal of life; who is free from all attachment and has love for all that I have created; that man comes to truly know who I am.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच । एवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते ये चाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः ॥...
hindi: अर्जुन बोले- जो अनन्यप्रेमी भक्तजन पूर्वोक्त प्रकारसे निरन्तर आपके भजन-ध्यानमें लगे रहकर आप सगुणरूप परमेश्वरको और दूसरे जो केवल अविनाशी सच्चिदानन्दघन निराकार ब्रह्मको ही अतिश्रेष्ठ भावसे भजते हैं – उन दोनों प्रकारके – उपासकोंमें अति उत्तम योगवेत्ता कौन हैं ?
english: Arjuna asked: Those devotees who worship you as the one without attributes (formless) and those who worship you as the one with attributes (with form); which of these are better versed in Yoga?
explanation: Arjuna asked: Those devotees who worship you as the one without attributes (formless) and those who worship you as the one with attributes (with form); which of these are better versed in Yoga?

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । मय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते श्रद्धया परयोपेतास्ते मे युक्ततमा मताः ॥ ...
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले—मुझमें मनको एकाग्र करके निरन्तर मेरे भजन-ध्यानमें लगे हुए जो भक्तजन अतिशय श्रेष्ठ श्रद्धासे युक्त होकर मुझ सगुणरूप परमेश्वरको भजते हैं, वे मुझको योगियोंमें अति उत्तम योगी मान्य हैं|
english: The Blessed Lord said: Those who have fixed their minds on Me and worship me with total dedication and faith, them I consider perfect in Yoga.
explanation: The Blessed Lord said: Those who have fixed their minds on Me and worship me with total dedication and faith, them I consider perfect in Yoga.

sanskrit: ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते । सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यं च कूटस्थमचलं ध्रुवम् ॥ १२.३ ॥
hindi: परन्तु जो पुरुष इन्द्रियोंके समुदायको भली प्रकार वशमें करके मन-बुद्धिसे परे, सर्वव्यापी, अकथनीयस्वरूप और सदा एकरस रहनेवाले, नित्य, अचल, निराकार, अविनाशी, सच्चिदानन्दघन ब्रह्मको निरन्तर एकीभावसे ध्यान करते हुए भजते हैं, वे सम्पूर्ण भूतोंके हितमें रत और सबमें समान भाववाले योगी मुझको ही प्राप्त होते हैं|
english: But those who worship the formless, the Indefinable, the Imperishable, the Unmanifest, the Omnipresent, the Unthinkable, the Unchangeable, the Immovable, the External- Having controlled all of the senses, always evenminded, thinking of the welfare of all beings, they also come to Me.
explanation: But those who worship the formless, the Indefinable, the Imperishable, the Unmanifest, the Omnipresent, the Unthinkable, the Unchangeable, the Immovable, the External- Having controlled all of the senses, always evenminded, thinking of the welfare of all beings, they also come to Me.

sanskrit: संनियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः । ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥ १२.४ ॥
hindi: परन्तु जो पुरुष इन्द्रियोंके समुदायको भली प्रकार वशमें करके मन-बुद्धिसे परे, सर्वव्यापी, अकथनीयस्वरूप और सदा एकरस रहनेवाले, नित्य, अचल, निराकार, अविनाशी, सच्चिदानन्दघन ब्रह्मको निरन्तर एकीभावसे ध्यान करते हुए भजते हैं, वे सम्पूर्ण भूतोंके हितमें रत और सबमें समान भाववाले योगी मुझको ही प्राप्त होते हैं|
english: But those who worship the formless, the Indefinable, the Imperishable, the Unmanifest, the Omnipresent, the Unthinkable, the Unchangeable, the Immovable, the External- Having controlled all of the senses, always evenminded, thinking of the welfare of all beings, they also come to Me.
explanation: But those who worship the formless, the Indefinable, the Imperishable, the Unmanifest, the Omnipresent, the Unthinkable, the Unchangeable, the Immovable, the External- Having controlled all of the senses, always evenminded, thinking of the welfare of all beings, they also come to Me.

sanskrit: क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम् । अव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दुःखं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते ॥ १२.५ ॥
hindi: न सच्चिदानन्दघन निराकार ब्रह्ममें आसक्त चित्तवाले पुरुषोंके साधनमें परिश्रम विशेष है; क्योंकि देहाभिमानियोंके द्वारा अव्यक्तविषयक गति दुःखपूर्वक प्राप्त की जाती है|
english: However, the worship of God without form is very difficult for the embodied because it is very hard to set the mind on the unmanifest, i.e. the one not capable of being recognized with the aid of the senses.
explanation: However, the worship of God without form is very difficult for the embodied because it is very hard to set the mind on the unmanifest, i.e. the one not capable of being recognized with the aid of the senses.

sanskrit: ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः । अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते ॥ १२.६ ॥
hindi: परन्तु जो मेरे परायण रहनेवाले भक्तजन सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंको मुझमें अर्पण करके मुझ सगुणरूप परमेश्वरको ही अनन्य भक्तियोगसे निरन्तर चिन्तन करते हुए भजते हैं|
english: But those who worship Me, dedicating all actions to Me, regarding Me as the Supreme Goal, meditating on Me with single-mindness.
explanation: But those who worship Me, dedicating all actions to Me, regarding Me as the Supreme Goal, meditating on Me with single-mindness.

sanskrit: तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात् । भवामि न चिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम् ॥ १२.७ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! उन मुझमें चित्त लगानेवाले प्रेमी भक्तोंका मैं शीघ्र ही मृत्युरूप संसार-समुद्रसे उद्धार करनेवाला होता हूँ|
english: These worshippers’ thoughts are set on Me; hence O Arjuna, I become their saviour from the wheel of birth and death.
explanation: These worshippers’ thoughts are set on Me; hence O Arjuna, I become their saviour from the wheel of birth and death.

sanskrit: मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय । निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः ॥ १२.८ ॥
hindi: मुझमें मनको लगा और मुझमें ही बुद्धिको लगा; इसके उपरान्त तू मुझमें ही निवास करेगा, इसमें कुछ भी संशय नहीं है|
english: Therefore, fix your mind on Me alone, let your thoughts reside in Me. You will hereafter live in Me alone. Do not have any doubt about it.
explanation: Therefore, fix your mind on Me alone, let your thoughts reside in Me. You will hereafter live in Me alone. Do not have any doubt about it.

sanskrit: अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम् । अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनंजय ॥ १२.९ ॥
hindi: यदि तू मनको मुझमें अचल स्थापन करनेके लिये समर्थ नहीं है तो हे अर्जुन! अभ्यासरूप योगके द्वारा मुझको प्राप्त होनेके लिये इच्छा कर|
english: If you are not able to set your mind on Me, O Arjuna, then wish to reach Me by the Yoga of constant practice.
explanation: If you are not able to set your mind on Me, O Arjuna, then wish to reach Me by the Yoga of constant practice.

sanskrit: अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः । सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ॥ १२.११ ॥
hindi: यदि मेरी प्राप्तिरूप योगके आश्रित होकर उपर्युक्त साधनको करनेमें भी तू असमर्थ है तो मन बुद्धि – आदिपर विजय प्राप्त करनेवाला होकर सब कर्मोंके फलका त्याग कर|
english: If you cannot do actions for My sake, then taking refuge in Me, renounce (abandon) all the fruits of action by controlling yourself.
explanation: If you cannot do actions for My sake, then taking refuge in Me, renounce (abandon) all the fruits of action by controlling yourself.

sanskrit: श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते । ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम् ॥ १२...
hindi: मर्मको न जानकर किये हुए अभ्याससे ज्ञान श्रेष्ठ है; ज्ञानसे मुझ परमेश्वरके स्वरूपका ध्यान श्रेष्ठ है और ध्यानसे भी सब कर्मोंके फलका त्याग श्रेष्ठ है; क्योंकि त्यागसे तत्काल ही परम शान्ति होती है|
english: To gain spiritual knowledge is better than to practise Yoga; Meditation is better than Knowledge, abandoning the fruit of action is better than meditation because peace immediately follows the abandoning of the fruits of action.
explanation: To gain spiritual knowledge is better than to practise Yoga; Meditation is better than Knowledge, abandoning the fruit of action is better than meditation because peace immediately follows the abandoning of the fruits of action.

sanskrit: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च । निर्ममो निरहंकारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥ १२.१३ ॥
hindi: जो पुरुष सब भूतोंमें द्वेषभावसे रहित, स्वार्थरहित, सबका प्रेमी और हेतुरहित दयालु है तथा ममतासे रहित, अहंकारसे रहित, सुख-दुःखोंकी प्राप्तिमें सम और क्षमावान् है अर्थात् अपराध करनेवालेको भी अभय देनेवाला है; तथा जो योगी निरन्तर संतुष्ट है, मन – इन्द्रियोंसहित शरीरको वशमें किये हुए है और मुझमें दृढ़ निश्चयवाला है- – वह मुझमें अर्पण किये हुए मन – बुद्धिवाला मेरा भक्त मुझको प्रिय है
english: The Lord describes the divine qualities of a devotee: He who hates no one, who is friendly and compassionate to all, who is free of egoism, balanced in pain and pleasure and forgiving. He who is ever content, ever steady in meditation, self-controlled and firmly committed with mind and intellect fixed on Me, that devotee is dear to Me.
explanation: The Lord describes the divine qualities of a devotee: He who hates no one, who is friendly and compassionate to all, who is free of egoism, balanced in pain and pleasure and forgiving. He who is ever content, ever steady in meditation, self-controlled and firmly committed with mind and intellect fixed on Me, that devotee is dear to Me.

sanskrit: संतुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः । मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥ १२.१४ ॥
hindi: जो पुरुष सब भूतोंमें द्वेषभावसे रहित, स्वार्थरहित, सबका प्रेमी और हेतुरहित दयालु है तथा ममतासे रहित, अहंकारसे रहित, सुख-दुःखोंकी प्राप्तिमें सम और क्षमावान् है अर्थात् अपराध करनेवालेको भी अभय देनेवाला है; तथा जो योगी निरन्तर संतुष्ट है, मन – इन्द्रियोंसहित शरीरको वशमें किये हुए है और मुझमें दृढ़ निश्चयवाला है- – वह मुझमें अर्पण किये हुए मन – बुद्धिवाला मेरा भक्त मुझको प्रिय है|
english: The Lord describes the divine qualities of a devotee: He who hates no one, who is friendly and compassionate to all, who is free of egoism, balanced in pain and pleasure and forgiving. He who is ever content, ever steady in meditation, self-controlled and firmly committed with mind and intellect fixed on Me, that devotee is dear to Me.
explanation: The Lord describes the divine qualities of a devotee: He who hates no one, who is friendly and compassionate to all, who is free of egoism, balanced in pain and pleasure and forgiving. He who is ever content, ever steady in meditation, self-controlled and firmly committed with mind and intellect fixed on Me, that devotee is dear to Me.

sanskrit: यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः । हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥ १२.१५ ॥
hindi: जिससे कोई भी जीव उद्वेगको प्राप्त नहीं होता और जो स्वयं भी किसी जीवसे उद्वेगको प्राप्त नहीं होता; तथा जो हर्ष, अमर्ष, भय और उद्वेगादिसे रहित है – वह भक्त मुझको प्रिय है|
english: He who does not harm anyone in the world and from whom the world is not agitated, and he who knows no joy, envy, fear and anxiety, that devotee is dear to me.
explanation: He who does not harm anyone in the world and from whom the world is not agitated, and he who knows no joy, envy, fear and anxiety, that devotee is dear to me.

sanskrit: अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः । सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥ १२.१६ ॥
hindi: जो पुरुष आकांक्षासे रहित, बाहर भीतरसे – शुद्ध चतुर, पक्षपातसे रहित और दुःखोंसे छूटा २ हुआ है – वह सब आरम्भोंका त्यागी मेरा भक्त मुझको प्रिय है|
english: He who is free from desires, who is pure, expert, unconcerned, free from pain, selfless in all of his actions, he who is thus devoted to Me, is dear to Me.
explanation: He who is free from desires, who is pure, expert, unconcerned, free from pain, selfless in all of his actions, he who is thus devoted to Me, is dear to Me.

sanskrit: यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति । शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः ॥ १२.१७ ॥
hindi: जो न कभी हर्षित होता है, न द्वेष करता है, न शोक करता है, न कामना करता है तथा जो शुभ और अशुभ सम्पूर्ण कर्मोंका त्यागी है – वह भक्तियुक्त पुरुष मुझको प्रिय है|
english: He who neither rejoices (obtaining the desirable objects), nor hates (the undesirable objects), nor grieves (losing his beloved objects), nor desires (his beloved objects), free from the notions of good and evil, full of devotion, he is dear to Me.
explanation: He who neither rejoices (obtaining the desirable objects), nor hates (the undesirable objects), nor grieves (losing his beloved objects), nor desires (his beloved objects), free from the notions of good and evil, full of devotion, he is dear to Me.

sanskrit: समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः । शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ॥ १२.१८ ॥
hindi: जो शत्रु मित्रमें और मान-अपमानमें सम है – तथा सरदी, गरमी और सुख-दुःखादि द्वन्द्वोंमें सम है और आसक्तिसे रहित है|
english: He who is the same to enemy and friend and also in honour and dishonour, in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, and who is free from attachment-
explanation: He who is the same to enemy and friend and also in honour and dishonour, in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, and who is free from attachment-

sanskrit: तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित् । अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः ॥ १२.१९ ॥
hindi: जो निन्दा – स्तुतिको समान समझनेवाला, मननशील और जिस किसी प्रकारसे भी शरीरका निर्वाह होनेमें सदा ही सन्तुष्ट है और रहनेके स्थानमें ममता और आसक्तिसे रहित है – वह स्थिरबुद्धि भक्तिमान् पुरुष मुझको प्रिय है|
english: He who is neutral in censure and praise, He who is silent, content with anything (in the world), does not claim any residence as his home, he who is steady-minded, full of devotion; that man is dear to Me.
explanation: He who is neutral in censure and praise, He who is silent, content with anything (in the world), does not claim any residence as his home, he who is steady-minded, full of devotion; that man is dear to Me.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच । प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव...
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले- हे अर्जुन ! यह शरीर ‘क्षेत्र १ इस नामसे कहा जाता है और इसको जो जानता है, उसको ‘क्षेत्रज्ञ’ इस नामसे उनके तत्त्वको जाननेवाले ज्ञानीजन कहते हैं|
english: The Blessed Lord said: This body, O Arjuna, is called Kshetra (the field); he who knows it is called Kshetragya (the knower of the field) by the sages (those who have acquired spiritual knowledge).
explanation: The Blessed Lord said: This body, O Arjuna, is called Kshetra (the field); he who knows it is called Kshetragya (the knower of the field) by the sages (those who have acquired spiritual knowledge).

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्व...
hindi: धन्य प्रभु ने कहा: इस शरीर, हे कौंटेया को क्षेत्र (क्षेत्र) कहा जाता है और जो इसे जानता है, उसे उन लोगों द्वारा क्षेत्रज्ना (क्षेत्र का ज्ञाता) कहा जाता है जो उन्हें जानते हैं (क्षेत्र का ज्ञानी) अर्थात्, ऋषियों द्वारा।
english: The Blessed Lord said: This body, O Kaunteya is called KSHETRA (the Field) and he who knows it is called KSHETRAJNA (the Knower-of-the-Field) by those who know them (KSHETRA and KSHETRAJNA) i. e. , by the sages .
explanation: The Blessed Lord said: This body, O Kaunteya is called KSHETRA (the Field) and he who knows it is called KSHETRAJNA (the Knower-of-the-Field) by those who know them (KSHETRA and KSHETRAJNA) i. e. , by the sages .

sanskrit: क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत । क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥...
hindi: हे अर्जुन! तू सब क्षेत्रों में क्षेत्रज्ञ अर्थात जीवात्मा भी मुझे ही जान और क्षेत्र क्षेत्रज्ञको अर्थात् विकारसहित प्रकृतिका और पुरुषका जो तत्त्व जानना है, वह ज्ञान है – ऐसा मेरा मत है|
english: And you should know Me as the knower of the field in all fields, O Arjuna. The knowledge of both the field and the knower of the field is considered by Me to be real knowledge.
explanation: And you should know Me as the knower of the field in all fields, O Arjuna. The knowledge of both the field and the knower of the field is considered by Me to be real knowledge.

sanskrit: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् । स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे शृणु ॥ १३.४ ॥
hindi: वह क्षेत्र जो और जैसा है तथा जिन विकारोंवाला है, और जिस कारणसे जो हुआ है; तथा वह क्षेत्रज्ञ भी जो और जिस प्रभाववाला है – वह सब संक्षेपमें – मुझसे सुन|
english: What the field is, what it is like, what is its nature, what are its properties and modifications, from what causes, and also, who He (the knower of the field) is, and what His powers are; hear briefly all that from Me.
explanation: What the field is, what it is like, what is its nature, what are its properties and modifications, from what causes, and also, who He (the knower of the field) is, and what His powers are; hear briefly all that from Me.

sanskrit: ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक् । ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितैः ॥ १३.५ ॥
hindi: ऋषि ने विभिन्न विशिष्ट मंत्रों में कई मायनों में (क्षेत्र और क्षेत्र के ज्ञाता के बारे में) गाया है और तर्क और निर्णय से भरे ब्रह्म के सूचक शब्दों में भी गाया है।
english: RISHIS have sung (about the Field and the Knower-of-the-Field ) in many ways in various distinctive chants and also in the suggestive words indicative of BRAHMAN full of reason and decision.
explanation: RISHIS have sung (about the Field and the Knower-of-the-Field ) in many ways in various distinctive chants and also in the suggestive words indicative of BRAHMAN full of reason and decision.

sanskrit: महाभूतान्यहंकारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च । इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः ॥ १३.६ ॥
hindi: महान तत्व, अहंकारवाद, बुद्धि, और यह भी अप्रकट (मूला-प्रकृति), दस इंद्रियों और एक (मन) और पांच वस्तुओं-की-इंद्रियों, ...
english: The great elements, egoism, intellect, and also the unmanifested (MOOLA-PRAKRITI) , the ten senses and the one (the mind) and the five objects-of-the-senses, . . .
explanation: The great elements, egoism, intellect, and also the unmanifested (MOOLA-PRAKRITI) , the ten senses and the one (the mind) and the five objects-of-the-senses, . . .

sanskrit: इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं संघातश्चेतना धृतिः । एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥ १३.७ ॥
hindi: तथा इच्छा, द्वेष, सुख, दुःख, स्थूल देहका पिण्ड चेतना’ और धृति९— इस प्रकार विकारों के सहित यह क्षेत्र संक्षेपमें कहा गया|
english: Desire, hatred, pleasure, pain, the body, intelligence, firmness; these along with their modifications have been called the field (Kshetra).
explanation: Desire, hatred, pleasure, pain, the body, intelligence, firmness; these along with their modifications have been called the field (Kshetra).

sanskrit: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् । आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः ॥ १३.८ ॥
hindi: श्रेष्ठताके अभिमानका अभाव, दम्भाचरणका अभाव, किसी भी प्राणीको किसी प्रकार भी न सताना, क्षमाभाव, मन- -वाणी आदिकी सरलता, श्रद्धा-भक्तिसहित गुरुकी सेवा, बाहर भीतरकी शुद्धि, अन्तःकरणकी स्थिरता – और मन-इन्द्रियोंसहित शरीरका निग्रह|
english: Humility, modesty, non-injury, forgiveness, uprightness, service of the teacher, purity, steadfastness, self-control.
explanation: Humility, modesty, non-injury, forgiveness, uprightness, service of the teacher, purity, steadfastness, self-control.

sanskrit: इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहंकार एव च । जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम् ॥ १३.९ ॥
hindi: इस लोक और परलोकके सम्पूर्ण भोगोंमें आसक्तिका अभाव और अहंकारका भी अभाव, जन्म, मृत्यु, जरा और रोग आदिमें दुःख और दोषोंका बार बार विचार करना|
english: Indifference to the sense-objects (such as sound, touch, etc.);absence of egoism (e.g. I am superior to all); reflection on the evil in birth, death, old age, sickness and pain.
explanation: Indifference to the sense-objects (such as sound, touch, etc.);absence of egoism (e.g. I am superior to all); reflection on the evil in birth, death, old age, sickness and pain.

sanskrit: मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी । विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ॥ १३.११ ॥
hindi: मुझ परमेश्वरमें अनन्य योगके द्वारा अव्यभिचारिणी भक्ति तथा एकान्त और शुद्ध देशमें रहनेका स्वभाव और विषयासक्त मनुष्योंके समुदायमें प्रेमका न होना|
english: Unflinching devotion to Me by the Yoga of non-separation, resort to solitary places, distaste for the society of worldly-minded people.
explanation: Unflinching devotion to Me by the Yoga of non-separation, resort to solitary places, distaste for the society of worldly-minded people.

sanskrit: अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् । एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा ॥ १३.१२ ॥
hindi: अध्यात्मज्ञानमें नित्यस्थिति और तत्त्वज्ञान के अर्थरूप परमात्माको ही देखना – यह सब ज्ञान’ है; और जो इससे विपरीत है, वह अज्ञान है – ऐसा – कहा है|
english: Constant awareness of the Self (self-knowledge),perception of the end of true knowledge – that is declared to be the true knowledge, and what is opposed to it is ignorance.
explanation: Constant awareness of the Self (self-knowledge),perception of the end of true knowledge – that is declared to be the true knowledge, and what is opposed to it is ignorance.

sanskrit: ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते । अनादि मत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ॥ १३.१३ ॥
hindi: जो जाननेयोग्य है तथा जिसको जानकर मनुष्य परमानन्दको प्राप्त होता है, उसको भलीभाँति कहूँगा। वह अनादिवाला परमब्रह्म न सत् ही कहा जाता है, न असत् ही|
english: I shall now state that which has to be known, knowing which one attains to immortality; the Supreme Brahman is beginningless and he is called neither ‘Sat’ (being) nor ‘Asat’ (non-being).
explanation: I shall now state that which has to be known, knowing which one attains to immortality; the Supreme Brahman is beginningless and he is called neither ‘Sat’ (being) nor ‘Asat’ (non-being).

sanskrit: सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् । सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ॥ १३.१४ ॥
hindi: वह सब ओर हाथ-पैरवाला, सब ओर नेत्र, सिर और मुखवाला तथा सब ओर कानवाला है। क्योंकि वह संसारमें सबको व्याप्त करके स्थित है ?
english: With hands and feet everywhere, with eyes, heads and mouths everywhere, with ears everywhere, He (the knower of the field) exists enveloping all.
explanation: With hands and feet everywhere, with eyes, heads and mouths everywhere, with ears everywhere, He (the knower of the field) exists enveloping all.

sanskrit: सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् । असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ॥ १३.१५ ॥
hindi: वह सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियोंके विषयोंको जाननेवाला है, परन्तु वास्तवमें सब इन्द्रियोंसे रहित है तथा आसक्ति रहित होनेपर भी सबका धारण-पोषण करनेवाला और निर्गुण होनेपर भी गुणोंको भोगनेवाला है|
english: Shining by the functions of all the senses (see previous verses for the names of the senses), yet without the senses (i.e. organs), unattached, yet supporting all. devoid of Gunas (qualities), yet He experiences them.
explanation: Shining by the functions of all the senses (see previous verses for the names of the senses), yet without the senses (i.e. organs), unattached, yet supporting all. devoid of Gunas (qualities), yet He experiences them.

sanskrit: बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च । सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ॥ १३.१६ ॥
hindi: वह चराचर सब भूतोंके बाहर भीतर परिपूर्ण – है और चर-अचर भी वही है । और वह सूक्ष्म होनेसे अविज्ञेय’ है तथा अति समीपमें और दूरमें भी स्थित वही है|
english: He is outside and inside all beings; the unmoving and also the moving ; because of His subtlety (like ether), he is unknowable. He is far and near.
explanation: He is outside and inside all beings; the unmoving and also the moving ; because of His subtlety (like ether), he is unknowable. He is far and near.

sanskrit: अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् । भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ॥ १३.१७ ॥
hindi: वह परमात्मा विभागरहित एक रूपसे आकाशके सदृश परिपूर्ण होनेपर भी चराचर सम्पूर्ण भूतोंमें विभक्त-सा स्थित प्रतीत होता है तथा वह जाननेयोग्य परमात्मा विष्णुरूपसे भूतोंको धारण पोषण करनेवाला और रुद्ररूपसे संहार करनेवाला तथा ब्रह्मारूपसे सबको उत्पन्न करनेवाला है|
english: He is undivided and yet he appears to be divided in beings. He supports, swallows up and also creates all beings.
explanation: He is undivided and yet he appears to be divided in beings. He supports, swallows up and also creates all beings.

sanskrit: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते । ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ॥ १३.१८ ॥
hindi: वह परब्रह्म ज्योतियोंका भी ज्योति एवं मायासे अत्यन्त परे कहा जाता है । वह परमात्मा बोधस्वरूप, जाननेके योग्य एवं तत्त्वज्ञानसे प्राप्त करनेयोग्य है और सबके हृदयमें विशेषरूपसे स्थित है|
english: He is light of all lights and is said to be beyond darkness. He is Knowledge, the Knowable (that which has to be known) and the goal of Knowledge, and He is seated in the hearts of all.
explanation: He is light of all lights and is said to be beyond darkness. He is Knowledge, the Knowable (that which has to be known) and the goal of Knowledge, and He is seated in the hearts of all.

sanskrit: इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः । मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ॥ १३.१९ ॥
hindi: इस प्रकार क्षेत्र’ तथा ज्ञान और जाननेयोग्य परमात्माका स्वरूप संक्षेपसे कहा गया। मेरा ३ भक्त इसको तत्त्वसे जानकर मेरे स्वरूपको प्राप्त होता है|
english: Thus the field, knowledge and the knowable have been briefly stated (by Me). My devotee, on knowing this, becomes one with Me.
explanation: Thus the field, knowledge and the knowable have been briefly stated (by Me). My devotee, on knowing this, becomes one with Me.

sanskrit: कार्यकरणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते । पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ॥ १३.२१ ॥
hindi: कार्य और करण को उत्पन्न करनेमें हेतु प्रकृति कही जाती है और जीवात्मा सुख-दुःखोंके भोक्तापनमें अर्थात् भोगनेमें हेतु कहा जाता है|
english: Both the effect and the cause are generated from nature, and the spirit (soul) is the cause in the experience of pain and pleasure.
explanation: Both the effect and the cause are generated from nature, and the spirit (soul) is the cause in the experience of pain and pleasure.

sanskrit: पुरुष: प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङक्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् । कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥ १३.२२...
hindi: भावार्थ : कार्य (आकाश, वायु, अग्नि, जल और पृथ्वी तथा शब्द, स्पर्श, रूप, रस, गंध -इनका नाम ‘कार्य’ है) और करण (बुद्धि, अहंकार और मन तथा श्रोत्र, त्वचा, रसना, नेत्र और घ्राण एवं वाक्‌, हस्त, पाद, उपस्थ और गुदा- इन 13 का नाम ‘करण’ है) को उत्पन्न करने में हेतु प्रकृति कही जाती है और जीवात्मा सुख-दुःखों के भोक्तपन में अर्थात भोगने में हेतु कहा जाता है|
english: The spirit (soul) residing in nature experiences the qualities born of nature; this attachment to the qualities is the cause of his birth in pure (good) and impure (evil) wombs.
explanation: The spirit (soul) residing in nature experiences the qualities born of nature; this attachment to the qualities is the cause of his birth in pure (good) and impure (evil) wombs.

sanskrit: उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः । परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः ॥ १३.२३ ॥
hindi: इस देह में स्थित यह आत्मा वास्तव में परमात्मा ही है । वही साक्षी होने से उपद्रष्टा और यथार्थ सम्मति देने वाला होने से अनुमन्ता, सबका धारण-पोषण करने वाला होने से भर्ता, जीव रूप से भोक्त्ता, ब्रह्मा आदि का भी स्वामी होने से महेश्वर और शुद्ध सच्चिदानन्दधन होने से परमात्मा —-ऐसा कहा गया है |
english: The Supreme Soul in this body is also called the spectator, the permitter, the supporter, the enjoyer, the Great Lord and the Supreme Self (Parmatma).
explanation: The Supreme Soul in this body is also called the spectator, the permitter, the supporter, the enjoyer, the Great Lord and the Supreme Self (Parmatma).

sanskrit: य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैः सह । सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥ १३.२४ ॥
hindi: इस प्रकार पुरुषों को और गुणों के सहित प्रकृति को जो मनुष्य तत्व से जानता है, वह सब प्रकार से कर्तव्य कर्म करता हुआ भी फिर नहीं जन्मता|
english: He who thus knows the Soul and Nature with the qualities, he is never born again regardless of the conditions he lives in.
explanation: He who thus knows the Soul and Nature with the qualities, he is never born again regardless of the conditions he lives in.

sanskrit: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना । अन्ये सांख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ॥ १३.२५ ॥
hindi: उस परमात्मा को तो कितने ही मनुष्य तो शुद्ध हुई सूक्ष्म बुद्भि से ध्यान के द्वारा ह्रदय में देखते है, अन्य कितने ही ज्ञान योग के द्वारा और दूसरे कितने ही कर्मयोग के द्वारा देखते हैं अर्थात् प्राप्त करते हैं|
english: Some by Yoga of meditation, behold the Self (supreme) in the self (intellect) by the self (purified mind); others by the Yoga of knowledge, and yet others by the Yoga of action.
explanation: Some by Yoga of meditation, behold the Self (supreme) in the self (intellect) by the self (purified mind); others by the Yoga of knowledge, and yet others by the Yoga of action.

sanskrit: अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते । तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः ॥ १३.२६ ॥
hindi: परन्तु इनसे दूसरे, अर्थात् जो मन्द बुद्भि वाले पुरुष हैं ; वे इस प्रकार न जानते हुए दूसरों से अर्थात् तत्व के जानने वाले पुरुषों से सुनकर ही तदनुसार उपासना करते है और वे श्रवण परायण पुरुष भी मृत्यु रूप संसार सागर को नि:संदेह तर जाते हैं |
english: Those who may not know Me by other means, if worshipping Me as they have heard from others, they too go beyond death by their devotion to what they have heard.
explanation: Those who may not know Me by other means, if worshipping Me as they have heard from others, they too go beyond death by their devotion to what they have heard.

sanskrit: यावत्संजायते किंचित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् । क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥ १३.२७ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! यावन्मात्र जितने भी स्थावर-जंगम प्राणी उत्पन्न होते हैं, उन सबको तू क्षेत्र और क्षेत्रज्ञ के संयोग से ही उत्पन्न जान|
english: Whatever is born, unmoving or moving, O Arjuna, know it to be from the union of the field and its knower.
explanation: Whatever is born, unmoving or moving, O Arjuna, know it to be from the union of the field and its knower.

sanskrit: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् । विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥ १३.२८ ॥
hindi: जो पुरुष नष्ट होते हुए इस चराचर भूतों में परमेश्वर को नाशरहित और समभाव से स्थित देखता हैं, वही यथार्थ देखता हैं |
english: He who beholds the imperishable Supreme Lord, existing equally in all perishable beings, realizes the truth.
explanation: He who beholds the imperishable Supreme Lord, existing equally in all perishable beings, realizes the truth.

sanskrit: समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् । न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥ १३.२९ ॥
hindi: क्योंकि जो पुरुष सब में समभाव से स्थित परमेश्वर को समान देखता हुआ अपने द्वारा अपने को नष्ट नहीं करता, इससे वह परम गति को प्राप्त होता है |
english: Because he who sees the same Lord existing everywhere does not destroy the Self by the self (intellect); therefore, he goes to the highest goal (is released from the round of birth and death).
explanation: Because he who sees the same Lord existing everywhere does not destroy the Self by the self (intellect); therefore, he goes to the highest goal (is released from the round of birth and death).

sanskrit: यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति । तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म संपद्यते तदा ॥ १३.३१ ॥
hindi: जिस क्षण यह पुरुष भूतों के पृथक-पृथक भाव को एक परमात्मा में ही स्थित तथा उस परमात्मा से ही सम्पूर्ण भूतों का विस्तार देखता है, उसी क्षण वह सच्चिदानन्दधन ब्रह्म को प्राप्त हो जाता हैं |
english: When a man realizes that the whole variety of beings are residing in the One, and are an evolution from that One alone, then he becomes Brahman (united with the Supreme).
explanation: When a man realizes that the whole variety of beings are residing in the One, and are an evolution from that One alone, then he becomes Brahman (united with the Supreme).

sanskrit: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः । शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥ १३.३२ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! अनादि होने से और निर्गुण होने से यह अविनाशी परमात्मा शरीर में स्थित होने पर भी वास्तव में न तो कुछ करता है और न लिप्त ही होता है |
english: The Supreme Self without beginning, without qualities, imperishable, though dwelling in the body, O Arjuna, neither acts nor is attached to any action.
explanation: The Supreme Self without beginning, without qualities, imperishable, though dwelling in the body, O Arjuna, neither acts nor is attached to any action.

sanskrit: यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते । सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते ॥ १३.३३ ॥
hindi: जिस प्रकार सर्वत्र व्याप्त आकाश सूक्ष्म होने के कारण लिप्त नहीं होता, वैसे ही देह में सर्वत्र स्थित आत्मा निर्गुण होने के कारण देह के गुणों से लिप्त नहीं होता|
english: As the all pervading ether (sky) is not affected, by reasons of subtlety, so the Self (soul) seated in the body is not affected.
explanation: As the all pervading ether (sky) is not affected, by reasons of subtlety, so the Self (soul) seated in the body is not affected.

sanskrit: यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः । क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥ १३.३४ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! जिस प्रकार एक ही सूर्य इस सम्पूर्ण ब्रह्माण्ड को प्रकाशित करता है, उसी प्रकार एक ही आत्मा सम्पूर्ण क्षेत्र को प्रकाशित करता है|
english: Just as the one sun illuminates the whole world, so also the Lord of the field (Supreme Self) illuminates the whole field, O Arjuna.
explanation: Just as the one sun illuminates the whole world, so also the Lord of the field (Supreme Self) illuminates the whole field, O Arjuna.

sanskrit: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा । भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ॥ १३.३५ ॥
hindi: इस प्रकार क्षेत्र और क्षेत्रज्ञ के भेद को कार्य सहित तथा प्रकृति से मुक्त्त होने को जो पुरुष ज्ञान नेत्रों द्वारा तत्व से जानते हैं, वे महात्मा जन परम ब्रह्म परमात्मा को प्राप्त होते हैं |
english: They who know through the eye of intuition, this distinction between the field and its knower and also perceive the liberation from the Nature of being, go to the Supreme.
explanation: They who know through the eye of intuition, this distinction between the field and its knower and also perceive the liberation from the Nature of being, go to the Supreme.

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । परं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिम...
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले —-ज्ञानों में भी अति उत्तम उस परम ज्ञान को मैं फिर कहूँगा, जिसको जानकर सब मुनि जन इस संसार से मुक्त्त होकर परम सिद्भि को प्राप्त हो गये हैं |
english: The blessed Lord spoke: Now Arjuna, I shall expose to you the most supreme and highest of all the wisdom in this universe by which all the great sages in this universe have achieved the highest perfection.
explanation: The blessed Lord spoke: Now Arjuna, I shall expose to you the most supreme and highest of all the wisdom in this universe by which all the great sages in this universe have achieved the highest perfection.

sanskrit: इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः । सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥ १४.२ ॥
hindi: इस ज्ञान को आश्रय करके अर्थात धारण करके मेरे स्वरूप को प्राप्त हुए पुरुष सृष्टि के आदि में पुन: उत्पन्न नहीं होते और प्रलय काल में भी व्याकुल नहीं होते|
english: By fully learning, understanding and practising this wisdom, these sages have become a part of Me. As a result, they are not born at the time of creation nor do they pass away at the time of destruction of the universe.
explanation: By fully learning, understanding and practising this wisdom, these sages have become a part of Me. As a result, they are not born at the time of creation nor do they pass away at the time of destruction of the universe.

sanskrit: मम योनिर्महद्ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् । संभवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥ १४.३ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! मेरी महत्-ब्रह्म रूप मूल प्रकृति सम्पूर्ण भूतों की योनि है अर्थात् गर्भाधान का स्थान है और मैं उस योनि में चेतन समुदाय रूप गर्भ को स्थापन करता हूँ । उस जड़ चेतन के संयोग से सब भूतों की उत्पत्ति होती है |
english: O Arjuna, you must realize that it is from Me that all creation steam. The great Brahma, The Lord of all creation acts as My womb when I plant the seed of creation from which all beings evolve.
explanation: O Arjuna, you must realize that it is from Me that all creation steam. The great Brahma, The Lord of all creation acts as My womb when I plant the seed of creation from which all beings evolve.

sanskrit: सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः संभवन्ति याः । तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता ॥ १४.४ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! नाना प्रकार की सब योनियों में जितनी मूर्तियां अर्थात् शरीर धारी प्राणी उत्पन्न होते हैं, प्रकृति तो उन सब की गर्भ धारण करने वाली माता है और मै बीज को स्थापन करने वाला पिता हूँ |
english: The blessed Lord said:Dear Arjuna, know that whenever and wherever a being is born, I am his parents, that is, both his mother and father, who gave him life in this world.
explanation: The blessed Lord said:Dear Arjuna, know that whenever and wherever a being is born, I am his parents, that is, both his mother and father, who gave him life in this world.

sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसंभवाः । निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥ १४.५ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! सत्वगुण, रजोगुण, और तमोगुण —-ये प्रकृति से उत्पन्न तीनों गुण अविनाशी जीवात्मा को शरीर में बांधते हैं|
english: Arjuna, that NATURE is made of three parts, namely: SATTVA (the light representing goodness); RAJAS (fire representing passion), and TAMAS (darkness representing evil). These bind down and limit the human mortal bodies from reaching true perfection and achieving the state of infinite Spirit and immortality.
explanation: Arjuna, that NATURE is made of three parts, namely: SATTVA (the light representing goodness); RAJAS (fire representing passion), and TAMAS (darkness representing evil). These bind down and limit the human mortal bodies from reaching true perfection and achieving the state of infinite Spirit and immortality.

sanskrit: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् । सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥ १४.६ ॥
hindi: हे निष्पाप ! उन तीनों गुणों में सत्वगुण तो निर्मल होने के कारण प्रकाश करने वाला और विकार रहित है, वह सुख के सम्बन्ध से और ज्ञान के सम्बन्ध से अर्थात् उसके अभिमान से बांधता है |
english: Arjuna, understand these three natural elements: SATTVA, because it represents light and purity is a sign of good health, but binds the various beings in this world to worldly material happiness. This attachment also leads to lower knowledge, my friend.
explanation: Arjuna, understand these three natural elements: SATTVA, because it represents light and purity is a sign of good health, but binds the various beings in this world to worldly material happiness. This attachment also leads to lower knowledge, my friend.

sanskrit: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम् । तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥ १४.७ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! राग रूप रजोगुण को कामना और आसक्ति से उत्पन्न जान । वह इस जीवात्मा को कर्मो के और उनके फल के सम्बन्ध से बांधता है |
english: RAJAS, dear Arjuna, is that natural element representing passion which leads to material and worldly attachment. Rajas is known to bind the Soul of mortal men to action.
explanation: RAJAS, dear Arjuna, is that natural element representing passion which leads to material and worldly attachment. Rajas is known to bind the Soul of mortal men to action.

sanskrit: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् । प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ॥ १४.८ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन सब देहाभिमानियों को मोहित करने वाले तमोगुण को तो अज्ञान से उत्पन्न जान । वह इस जीवात्मा को प्रमाद, आलस्य और निद्रा के द्वारा बांधता है|
english: But you should also know O Arjuna, that darkness and evil born out of the TAMAS GUNA, exists mainly because of the ignorance of man in this world. TAMAS creates laziness and dullness. One loses the value of time and no longer desires to work or to take part in constuctive action.
explanation: But you should also know O Arjuna, that darkness and evil born out of the TAMAS GUNA, exists mainly because of the ignorance of man in this world. TAMAS creates laziness and dullness. One loses the value of time and no longer desires to work or to take part in constuctive action.

sanskrit: सत्त्वं सुखे संजयति रजः कर्मणि भारत । ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे संजयत्युत ॥ १४.९ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! सत्वगुण सुख में लगाता है और रजोगुण कर्म में तथा तमोगुण तो ज्ञान को ढककर प्रमाद में भी लगाता है |
english: In reality O son of Kunti, SATTVA (or Goodness) binds one to happiness; RAJAS leads to attachment to action; and TAMAS (evil) leads one to be lazy, dull, unproductive and cause negligence due to one’s ignorance.
explanation: In reality O son of Kunti, SATTVA (or Goodness) binds one to happiness; RAJAS leads to attachment to action; and TAMAS (evil) leads one to be lazy, dull, unproductive and cause negligence due to one’s ignorance.

sanskrit: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते । ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥ १४.११ ॥
hindi: जिस समय इस देह में तथा अन्त:करण और इन्द्रियों में चेतनता और विवेक शक्त्ति उत्पन्न होती है, उस समय ऐसा जानना चाहिये कि सत्वगुण बढ़ा है|
english: When the light of true knowledge and wisdom sorrounds and comes forth from one’s body, it is a sign that the SATTVA state of nature has occupied the body and taken over the person’s body, evidenced by his good actions (therefore he is known as a SATTVIC person).
explanation: When the light of true knowledge and wisdom sorrounds and comes forth from one’s body, it is a sign that the SATTVA state of nature has occupied the body and taken over the person’s body, evidenced by his good actions (therefore he is known as a SATTVIC person).

sanskrit: लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा । रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥ १४.१२ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! रजोगुण के बढ़ने पर लोभ, प्रवृत्ति, स्वार्थ बुद्भि से कर्मों का सकाम भाव से आरम्भ, अशान्ति और विषय भोगों की लालसा ——ये सब उत्पन्न होते हैं |
english: When the RAJAS GUNA has taken over a being, then it can be seen through a person’s excessive greed, lust, unrest, activity and other similar action (therefore the person is known to be RAJAS).
explanation: When the RAJAS GUNA has taken over a being, then it can be seen through a person’s excessive greed, lust, unrest, activity and other similar action (therefore the person is known to be RAJAS).

sanskrit: अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च । तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥ १४.१३ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! तमोगुण के बढ़ने पर अन्त:करण और इन्द्रियों में अप्रकाश, कर्तव्य कर्मों में अप्रवृति और प्रमाद अर्थात् व्यर्थ चेष्टा और निद्रादि, अन्त:करण की मोहिनी वृतियाँ — ये सब ही उत्पन्न होते हैं |
english: Dullness, inactivity, laziness, negligence, ignorance, and sheer delusion about the world are all signs that the TAMAS elements have taken control of the person’s actions, behaviour, and thinking.
explanation: Dullness, inactivity, laziness, negligence, ignorance, and sheer delusion about the world are all signs that the TAMAS elements have taken control of the person’s actions, behaviour, and thinking.

sanskrit: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् । तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥ १४.१४ ॥
hindi: जब यह गुण सत्वगुण की वृद्भि में मृत्यु को प्राप्त होता है, तब तो उत्तम कार्य करने वालों के निर्मल दिव्य स्वर्गादि लोकों को प्राप्त होता है|
english: If a being is SATTVIC at the time that his soul departs from his body, his soul goes to the purest regions of the universe where truth and goodness prevail. He is therefore reborn at these same, pure regions of the earth O Arjuna.
explanation: If a being is SATTVIC at the time that his soul departs from his body, his soul goes to the purest regions of the universe where truth and goodness prevail. He is therefore reborn at these same, pure regions of the earth O Arjuna.

sanskrit: रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते । तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥ १४.१५ ॥
hindi: रजोगुण के बढ़ने पर मृत्यु को प्राप्त होकर कर्मों की आसक्ति वाले मनुष्यों में उत्पन्न होता है ; तथा तमोगुण के बढ़ने पर मरा हुआ मनुष्य कीट, पशु आदि मूढ़ योनियों में उत्पन्न होता है ।
english: Should a person be in the RAJAS state at the time of death, he is reborn in the places of the world where he is restless and constantly active. Similarly if a person is TAMASIC at the time of his death, he is reborn into a family of irrational behaviour, laziness and dullness.
explanation: Should a person be in the RAJAS state at the time of death, he is reborn in the places of the world where he is restless and constantly active. Similarly if a person is TAMASIC at the time of his death, he is reborn into a family of irrational behaviour, laziness and dullness.

sanskrit: कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् । रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ॥ १४.१६ ॥
hindi: श्रेष्ठ कर्म का तो सात्विक अर्थात् सुख, ज्ञान और वैराग्यादि निर्मल फल कहा है ; राजस कर्म का फल दुःख एवं तामस कर्म का फल अज्ञान कहा है ।
english: The purity of SATTVA is characterized by the rewards that come out of good actions. However, the fruits of work that is done in the RAJAS state brings nothing but pain to a person; and work that is done in the TAMAS state, bears fruits of dullness and ignorance.
explanation: The purity of SATTVA is characterized by the rewards that come out of good actions. However, the fruits of work that is done in the RAJAS state brings nothing but pain to a person; and work that is done in the TAMAS state, bears fruits of dullness and ignorance.

sanskrit: सत्त्वात्संजायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च । प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥ १४.१७ ॥
hindi: सत्वगुण से ज्ञान उत्पन्न होता है और रजोगुण से निस्संदेह लोभ तथा तमोगुण से प्रमाद और मोह उत्पन्न होते है और अज्ञान भी होता है ।
english: From the SATTVIC state of human nature one receives wisdom, from the RAJAS state arises greed, and form TAMAS evolves dullness and ignorance.
explanation: From the SATTVIC state of human nature one receives wisdom, from the RAJAS state arises greed, and form TAMAS evolves dullness and ignorance.

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः । जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः ॥ १४.१८...
hindi: सत्वगुण में स्थित पुरुष स्वर्गादि उच्च लोकों को जाते हैं ; रजोगुण में स्थित राजस पुरुष मध्य में अर्थात् मनुष्य लोक में ही रहते हैं और तमोगुण के कार्य रूप निद्रा, प्रमाद और आलस्यादि में स्थित तामस पुरुष अधोगति अर्थात् कीट, पशु आदि नीच योनियों को तथा नरकों को प्राप्त होते हैं ।
english: Those who are in the SATTVA state always follow the highest path or level of wisdom and intelligence; those who are in the RAJAS state always follow the middle level of intelligence and wisdom; and those who are under the influence of TAMAS from of human nature, always stoop to the lowest level of wisdom and knowledge and continually sink downwards along this path of ignorance.
explanation: Those who are in the SATTVA state always follow the highest path or level of wisdom and intelligence; those who are in the RAJAS state always follow the middle level of intelligence and wisdom; and those who are under the influence of TAMAS from of human nature, always stoop to the lowest level of wisdom and knowledge and continually sink downwards along this path of ignorance.

sanskrit: नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति । गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥ १४.१९ ॥
hindi: जिस समय द्रष्टा तीनों गुणों के अतिरिक्त अन्य किसी को कर्ता नहीं देखता और तीनो गुणों से अत्यन्त परे सच्चिदानन्धन स्वरूप मुझ परमात्मा को तत्व से जानता है, उस समय वह मेरे स्वरूप को प्राप्त होता है ।
english: When a true wiseman and seer fully understands that these three parts of nature are virtually the three main stages of life in this vast universe, he begins to also realize that which lies beyond these three conditions of nature, that is Myself. Thus once this is realized by any being they become an eternal part of Me the SUPREME LORD.
explanation: When a true wiseman and seer fully understands that these three parts of nature are virtually the three main stages of life in this vast universe, he begins to also realize that which lies beyond these three conditions of nature, that is Myself. Thus once this is realized by any being they become an eternal part of Me the SUPREME LORD.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच । कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥...
hindi: अर्जुन बोले ! इन तीनों गुणों से अतीत पुरुष किन-किन लक्षणों से युक्त्त होता है और किस प्रकार के आचरणों वाला होता है ; तथा हे प्रभो ! मनुष्य किस उपाय से इन तीनों गुणों से अतीत होता है ।
english: Arjuna then asked the Almighty Lord: How can one know if a particular being has gone beyond or has transcended the three powers of nature O Krishna? What is his way of life and how does he manage to reach beyond the SATTVA, RAJAS, and TAMAS modes of nature?
explanation: Arjuna then asked the Almighty Lord: How can one know if a particular being has gone beyond or has transcended the three powers of nature O Krishna? What is his way of life and how does he manage to reach beyond the SATTVA, RAJAS, and TAMAS modes of nature?

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव न द्वेष्टि संप्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क...
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले —–हे अर्जुन ! जो पुरुष सत्वगुण के कार्य रूप प्रकाश को, और रजोगुण के कार्य रूप प्रवृति को तथा तमोगुण के कार्य रूप मोह को भी न तो प्रवृत्त होने पर न तो उनसे करता है और न निवृत्त होने पर उनकी आकांक्षा करता है ।
english: My dear Pandava (Arjuna), he who does not hate nor has any desire for light, (representing results which are born out of SATTVIC deeds), busy activity, and all that represents darkness whether they are near to him or far from him…
explanation: My dear Pandava (Arjuna), he who does not hate nor has any desire for light, (representing results which are born out of SATTVIC deeds), busy activity, and all that represents darkness whether they are near to him or far from him…

sanskrit: उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते । गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येव योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥ १४.२३ ॥
hindi: जो साक्षी के सदृश स्थित हुआ गुणों के द्वारा विचलित नहीं किया जा सकता और गुण ही गुणों में बरतते है —-ऐसा समझता हुआ जो सच्चिदानन्दधन परमात्मा में एकीभाव से स्थिर रहता है एवं उस स्थिति से कभी विचलित नहीं होता ।
english: The Lord further explained:…He who sits apart undisturbed and unaffected by varying conditions of the environment and observes the world around him knowing and fully understanding that it is the power of nature that acts on all beings in this world…
explanation: The Lord further explained:…He who sits apart undisturbed and unaffected by varying conditions of the environment and observes the world around him knowing and fully understanding that it is the power of nature that acts on all beings in this world…

sanskrit: समदुःखसुखः स्वस्थः समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः । तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः ॥ १४.२४ ॥
hindi: जो निरन्तर आत्म भाव में स्थित, दुःख-सुख को समान समझने वाला, मिट्टी, पत्थर और स्वर्ण में समान भाव वाला, ज्ञानी, प्रिय तथा अप्रिय को एक सा मानने वाला और अपनी निन्दा स्तुति में भी समान भाव वाला है ।
english: जो निरन्तर आत्म भाव में स्थित, दुःख-सुख को समान समझने वाला, मिट्टी, पत्थर और स्वर्ण में समान भाव वाला, ज्ञानी, प्रिय तथा अप्रिय को एक सा मानने वाला और अपनी निन्दा स्तुति में भी समान भाव वाला है ।
explanation: जो निरन्तर आत्म भाव में स्थित, दुःख-सुख को समान समझने वाला, मिट्टी, पत्थर और स्वर्ण में समान भाव वाला, ज्ञानी, प्रिय तथा अप्रिय को एक सा मानने वाला और अपनी निन्दा स्तुति में भी समान भाव वाला है ।

sanskrit: मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः । सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते ॥ १४.२५ ॥
hindi: जो मान और अपमान में सम है, मित्र और बैरी के पक्ष में भी सम है एवं सम्पूर्ण आरम्भों में कर्तापन के अभिमान से रहित है, वह पुरुष गुणातीत कहा जाता है ।
english: He who behaves the same in times of honour or in times of disgrace; who behaves the same with enemies and friends; and who has surrendered all desires for action as well as lust for rewards resulting from those actions.
explanation: He who behaves the same in times of honour or in times of disgrace; who behaves the same with enemies and friends; and who has surrendered all desires for action as well as lust for rewards resulting from those actions.

sanskrit: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते । स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥ १४.२६ ॥
hindi: और जो पुरुष अव्यभिचारी भक्त्ति योग के द्वारा मुझको निरन्तर भजता है, वह भी इन तीनों गुणों को भली भाँति लाँघकर सच्चिदानन्दधन ब्रह्म को प्राप्त होने के लिये योग्य बन जाता है ।
english: And he who serves Me with an unfailing devotion, he is fit for becoming Brahman because he is gone beyond all the qualities (Gunas).
explanation: And he who serves Me with an unfailing devotion, he is fit for becoming Brahman because he is gone beyond all the qualities (Gunas).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च । शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥ १४.२७ ॥
hindi: क्योंकि उस अविनाशी परब्रह्म का और अमृत का तथा नित्य धर्म का और अखण्ड एक रस आनन्द का आश्रय मैं हूँ ।
english: I am the abode of Brahman, the immortal, the immutable, the eternal (ever-lasting) dharma and absolute bliss.
explanation: I am the abode of Brahman, the immortal, the immutable, the eternal (ever-lasting) dharma and absolute bliss.

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । ऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥ ...
hindi: श्री भगवान् बोले —–आदिपुरुषपरमेश्वर रूप मूल वाले और ब्रह्मा रूप मुख्य शाखा वाले जिस संसार रूप पीपल के वृक्ष को अविनाशी कहते हैं, तथा वेद जिसक पत्ते कहे गये हैं —-उस संसार रूप वृक्ष को जो पुरुष मूल सहित तत्व से जानता है, वह वेद के तात्पर्य को जानने वाला है ।
english: The Dear Lord explained to Arjuna:O Arjuna, there exists an enormous, everlasting, and divinely pure tree known as the indestructable ASVAATTHAM (COSMIC TREE OF LIFE). The extraordinary things about this tree, is that it has its roots above at its peak and its branches are located at the lowest part of the tree. It’s pure, green leaves are the VEDAS (Holy Scriptures and Sacred Songs). He who believes and realizes this truth, also knows and understands the VEDAS.
explanation: The Dear Lord explained to Arjuna:O Arjuna, there exists an enormous, everlasting, and divinely pure tree known as the indestructable ASVAATTHAM (COSMIC TREE OF LIFE). The extraordinary things about this tree, is that it has its roots above at its peak and its branches are located at the lowest part of the tree. It’s pure, green leaves are the VEDAS (Holy Scriptures and Sacred Songs). He who believes and realizes this truth, also knows and understands the VEDAS.

sanskrit: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः । अधश्च मूलान्यनुसंततानि कर्मानुबन्धीनि...
hindi: उस संसार वृक्ष की तीनों गुणों रूप जल के द्वारा बढ़ी हुई एवं विषय भोग रूप कोपलों वाली देव, मनुष्य और तिर्यक् आदि योनि रूप शाखाएँ नीचे ऊपर सर्वत्र फैली हुई हैं तथा मनुष्य लोक में क़र्मो के अनुसार बाँधने वाली अहंता ममता और वासना रूप जड़ें भी नीचे और ऊपर सभी लोकों में व्याप्त हो रही हैं ।
english: Its branches extend both below and above, form the earth to heaven (and vice-versa). It is the great powers of nature that nourish the tree and give it life. Its ouds represent the sensual objects that inspire pleasure in all beings. The roots that stretch a far distance downward (from the heavens) into the world of man are bounded as firmly to the earth as man is bound to the mortal world because of his selfish actions and lust for material rewards.
explanation: Its branches extend both below and above, form the earth to heaven (and vice-versa). It is the great powers of nature that nourish the tree and give it life. Its ouds represent the sensual objects that inspire pleasure in all beings. The roots that stretch a far distance downward (from the heavens) into the world of man are bounded as firmly to the earth as man is bound to the mortal world because of his selfish actions and lust for material rewards.

sanskrit: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते नान्तो न चादिर्न च संप्रतिष्ठा । अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूल- मसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढ...
hindi: इस संसार वृक्ष का स्वरूप जैसा कहा है ; वैसा यहाँ विचार काल में नहीं पाया जाता ; क्योंकि न तो इसका आदि है और न अन्त है तथा न इसकी अच्छी प्रकार से स्थिति ही है इस लिये इस अहंता, ममता और वासना रूप अति दृढ.मूलों वाले संसार रूप पीपल के वृक्ष को दृढ. वैराग्य स्वरूप शस्त्र द्वारा काट कर —– ।
english: Man cannot see this celestial tree nor even perceive its greatness. Man cannot see its changing forms nor its beginning, its end, nor its foundations. The only way one may be able to free oneself from the roots of this tree (which have spread and created this world of material attachment), is to cut free from these roots with the mighty sword of non-attachment.
explanation: Man cannot see this celestial tree nor even perceive its greatness. Man cannot see its changing forms nor its beginning, its end, nor its foundations. The only way one may be able to free oneself from the roots of this tree (which have spread and created this world of material attachment), is to cut free from these roots with the mighty sword of non-attachment.

sanskrit: ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यं यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः । तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये यतः प्रवृत्त...
hindi: उसके पश्चात् उस परम पद रूप परमेश्वर को भली भाँति खोजना चाहिये, जिसमें गये हुए पुरुष फिर लौट कर संसार में नहीं आते और जिस परमेश्वर से इस पुरातन संसार वृक्ष की प्रवृति विस्तार को प्राप्त हुई है, उसी आदि पुरुष नारायण के मैं शरण हूँ —-इस प्रकार द्ढ़ निश्चय करके उस परमेश्वर का मनन और निदिध्यासन करना चाहिये ।
english: If a man finds and proceeds along this path of non-attachment, he can say that he has truly discovered the way by which he can attain peaceful refuge and shelter in the Eternal Spirit (the Great beginning of all things is form whom all creation has evolved (namely, LORD KRISHNA Himself).
explanation: If a man finds and proceeds along this path of non-attachment, he can say that he has truly discovered the way by which he can attain peaceful refuge and shelter in the Eternal Spirit (the Great beginning of all things is form whom all creation has evolved (namely, LORD KRISHNA Himself).

sanskrit: निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः । द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञै- र्गच्...
hindi: जिसका मान और मोह नष्ट हो गया है, जिन्होंने आसक्ति रूप दोष को जीत लिया है, जिनकी परमात्मा के स्वरूप में नित्य स्थिति है और जिनकी कामनाएँ पुर्ण रूप से नष्ट हो गयी है —–वे सुख-दुःख नामक द्बन्द्बों से विमुक्त्त ज्ञानी जन उस अविनाशी परम पद को प्राप्त होते हैं ।
english: A man whose thinking is pure and consists of no evil; a man who has totally freed himself from the bondages of pride and delusion; who has broken free from the chains of attachment and all material desires; who has surrendered all selfish desires; who has learned to direct full concentration of the mind on the Supreme Spirit; and who has liberated himself from the opposing feelings of pleasure and pain, always achieves a place in the divine and Eternal Abode (shelter) that is within Me, the Almighty.
explanation: A man whose thinking is pure and consists of no evil; a man who has totally freed himself from the bondages of pride and delusion; who has broken free from the chains of attachment and all material desires; who has surrendered all selfish desires; who has learned to direct full concentration of the mind on the Supreme Spirit; and who has liberated himself from the opposing feelings of pleasure and pain, always achieves a place in the divine and Eternal Abode (shelter) that is within Me, the Almighty.

sanskrit: न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः । यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥ १५.६ ॥
hindi: जिस परम पद को प्राप्त होकर मनुष्य लौट कर संसार में नहीं आते, उस स्वयं प्रकाश परम पद को न सूर्य प्रकाशित कर सकता है, न चन्द्रमा और न अग्नि ही ; वही मेरा परम धाम है ।
english: That place upon which the sun cannot cast its glorious radiance, where the moon can shed no light, and where no fire, burns is what is known as My Supreme Abode. Here only the radiant light of My Eternal Glory shines. Those who reach this sacred destination, never return to suffer in this world again O Arjuna.
explanation: That place upon which the sun cannot cast its glorious radiance, where the moon can shed no light, and where no fire, burns is what is known as My Supreme Abode. Here only the radiant light of My Eternal Glory shines. Those who reach this sacred destination, never return to suffer in this world again O Arjuna.

sanskrit: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः । मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥ १५.७ ॥
hindi: इस देह में यह सनातन जीवात्मा मेरा ही अंश है और वही इन प्रकृति में स्थित मन और पाँचों इन्द्रियों को आकर्षण करता है ।
english: The Blessed Lord confided:O Arjuna, in this world, a certain fraction of My Eternal Spirit taken birth. This fragment of My Supreme Soul attracts the five senses plus an additional sixth one known as the mind and resides within the human body, in the realm of nature, on earth.
explanation: The Blessed Lord confided:O Arjuna, in this world, a certain fraction of My Eternal Spirit taken birth. This fragment of My Supreme Soul attracts the five senses plus an additional sixth one known as the mind and resides within the human body, in the realm of nature, on earth.

sanskrit: शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः । गृहित्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥ १५.८ ॥
hindi: वायु गन्ध के स्थान से गन्ध को जैसे ग्रहण करके ले जाता है, वैसे ही देहादि का स्वामी जीवात्मा भी जिस शरीर का त्याग करता है, उससे इन मन सहित इन्द्रियों को ग्रहण करके फिर जिस शरीर को प्राप्त होता है ——उसमें जाता है ।
english: When I, the Lord of all the bodies that walk on this earth, enter a particular body. (and give it life and soul), and later when I leave this body (in the form of the body’s soul), I take the senses and the mind with Me and drift away like the wind that carries away sweet fragrances from their hidden sources within nature.
explanation: When I, the Lord of all the bodies that walk on this earth, enter a particular body. (and give it life and soul), and later when I leave this body (in the form of the body’s soul), I take the senses and the mind with Me and drift away like the wind that carries away sweet fragrances from their hidden sources within nature.

sanskrit: श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च । अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥ १५.९ ॥
hindi: यह जीवात्मा श्रोत्र, चक्षु और त्वचा को तथा रसना, घ्राण और मन को आश्रय करके ——अर्थात् इन सब के सहारे से ही विषयों का सेवन करता है ।
english: Therefore, O Arjuna, after I enter the body of a particular being in this world, as its soul, I bestow upon this being its five senses namely, its eyesight, hearing, touch, taste, and smell, as well as its mind and furthermore it is because of Me that these senses function and stay operable. Thus, a being is able to experience the various objects in this world through his senses.
explanation: Therefore, O Arjuna, after I enter the body of a particular being in this world, as its soul, I bestow upon this being its five senses namely, its eyesight, hearing, touch, taste, and smell, as well as its mind and furthermore it is because of Me that these senses function and stay operable. Thus, a being is able to experience the various objects in this world through his senses.

sanskrit: यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् । यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः ॥ १५.११ ॥
hindi: यत्न करने वाले योगी जन भी अपने ह्रदय में स्थित इस आत्मा को तत्व से जानते हैं ; किंतु जिन्होंने अपने अन्त:करण को शुद्ध नहीं किया है, ऐसे अज्ञानी जन तो यत्न करते रहने पर भी इस आत्मा को नहीं जानते ।
english: Those people who are pure at heart and are constantly striving to see Me, inevitably see Me, the Lord who dwells in their own heart. However those people who are impure at heart and in their minds never perceive Me, the Supreme Spirit, no matter how much they strive.
explanation: Those people who are pure at heart and are constantly striving to see Me, inevitably see Me, the Lord who dwells in their own heart. However those people who are impure at heart and in their minds never perceive Me, the Supreme Spirit, no matter how much they strive.

sanskrit: यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम् । यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ॥ १५.१२ ॥
hindi: सूर्य में स्थित जो तेज सम्पूर्ण जगत् को प्रकाशित करता है तथा जो तेज चन्द्रमा में है और जो अग्नि में है —उसको तू मेरा ही तेज जान ।
english: The Blessed Lord spoken in His Divine Voice: Arjuna, you should realize and always remember that the beautiful and overwhelming light that comes from the sun and illuminates the whole universe, the soft and splendrous light that is emitted by the moon, and the radiant glow of a fire, all come from within Me and exist because of me alone.
explanation: The Blessed Lord spoken in His Divine Voice: Arjuna, you should realize and always remember that the beautiful and overwhelming light that comes from the sun and illuminates the whole universe, the soft and splendrous light that is emitted by the moon, and the radiant glow of a fire, all come from within Me and exist because of me alone.

sanskrit: गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा । पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः ॥ १५.१३ ॥
hindi: और मैं ही पृथ्वी में प्रवेश करके अपनी शक्त्ति से सब भूतों को धारण करता हूँ और रस स्वरूप अर्थात् अमृत मय चन्द्रमा होकर सम्पूर्ण ओषधियों को अर्थात् वनस्पतियों को पुष्ट करता हूँ ।
english: Upon entering into this world from the Heavens, O Arjuna, it is My Divine Energy that gives life and support to all things on this earth. As such, My Glory is so Divine and Beautiful that I become the Sacred Some plant that nourishes all the other plants and herbs on this earth. (The Soma or Moon plant, as it is literally translated, contains syrup that is considered to be very nourishing and healthy.)
explanation: Upon entering into this world from the Heavens, O Arjuna, it is My Divine Energy that gives life and support to all things on this earth. As such, My Glory is so Divine and Beautiful that I become the Sacred Some plant that nourishes all the other plants and herbs on this earth. (The Soma or Moon plant, as it is literally translated, contains syrup that is considered to be very nourishing and healthy.)

sanskrit: अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः । प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् ॥ १५.१४ ॥
hindi: मैं ही सब प्राणियों के शरीर के स्थित रहने वाला प्राण और अपान से संयुक्त्त वैश्वानर अग्निरूप होकर चार प्रकार के अन्न को पचाता हूँ ।
english: O Arjuna, at the same time, I also become the fire of life that exists in all things that breathe on this earth. Together, with the fiery breaths of life that flow in and flow out of body, I burn and digest the four kinds of pure foods on this earth, while residing in a being’s body.
explanation: O Arjuna, at the same time, I also become the fire of life that exists in all things that breathe on this earth. Together, with the fiery breaths of life that flow in and flow out of body, I burn and digest the four kinds of pure foods on this earth, while residing in a being’s body.

sanskrit: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि संनिविष्टो मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च । वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो वेदान्तकृद्व...
hindi: मैं ही सब प्राणियों के शरीर के स्थित रहने वाला प्राण और अपान से संयुक्त्त वैश्वानर अग्निरूप होकर चार प्रकार के अन्न को पचाता हूँ ।
english: O Arjuna, at the same time, I also become the fire of life that exists in all things that breathe on this earth. Together, with the fiery breaths of life that flow in and flow out of body, I burn and digest the four kinds of pure foods on this earth, while residing in a being’s body.
explanation: O Arjuna, at the same time, I also become the fire of life that exists in all things that breathe on this earth. Together, with the fiery breaths of life that flow in and flow out of body, I burn and digest the four kinds of pure foods on this earth, while residing in a being’s body.

sanskrit: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च । क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥ १५.१६ ॥
hindi: इस संसार में नाशवान् और अविनाशी भी ये दो प्रकार के पुरुष हैं । इनमे सम्पूर्ण भूत प्राणियों के शरीर तो नाशवान् और जीवात्मा अविनाशी कहा जाता है ।
english: There are two souls or spirits in this universe O Arjuna, namely, the destructible and indestructible. The destructible are all the those souls that are living in this universe and the indestructible are those souls that can never be subjected to change and are steady in their devotion to Me (therefore achieving immortality).
explanation: There are two souls or spirits in this universe O Arjuna, namely, the destructible and indestructible. The destructible are all the those souls that are living in this universe and the indestructible are those souls that can never be subjected to change and are steady in their devotion to Me (therefore achieving immortality).

sanskrit: उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः । यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः ॥ १५.१७ ॥
hindi: इन दोनों से उत्तम पुरुष तो अन्य ही है, जो तीनों लोकों में प्रवेश करके सबका धारण-पोषण करता है एवं अविनाशी परमेश्वर और परमात्मा इस प्रकार गया है ।
english: However dear Arjuna, there is another spirit that is the highest of all spirits and it is called the Supreme Spirit. The Supreme Spirit is none other than Me, the Eternal and Omniscient (existing everywhere) Lord who supports this entire universe.
explanation: However dear Arjuna, there is another spirit that is the highest of all spirits and it is called the Supreme Spirit. The Supreme Spirit is none other than Me, the Eternal and Omniscient (existing everywhere) Lord who supports this entire universe.

sanskrit: यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः । अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥ १५.१८ ॥
hindi: क्योंकि मैं नाशवान् जड. वर्ग क्षेत्र से तो सर्वथा अतीत हूँ और अविनाशी जीवात्मा से भी उत्तम हूँ, इसलिये लोक में और वेद में भी पुरुषोत्तम नाम से प्रसिद्ध हूँ ।
english: Lord Krishna explained:Because I am beyond all perishable objects on earth and because I even surpass all that is imperishable, I am known throughout the world and in all the Holy Scriptures (Vedas), as the Supreme and Divine Spirit.
explanation: Lord Krishna explained:Because I am beyond all perishable objects on earth and because I even surpass all that is imperishable, I am known throughout the world and in all the Holy Scriptures (Vedas), as the Supreme and Divine Spirit.

sanskrit: यो मामेवमसंमूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम् । स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत ॥ १५.१९ ॥
hindi: भारत ! जो ज्ञानी पुरुष मुझको इस प्रकार तत्व से पुरुषोतम जानता है, वह सर्वज्ञ पुरुष सब प्रकार से निरन्तर मुझ वासुदेव परमेश्वर को ही भजता है ।
english: He who has a clear vision and is constantly occupied with only divine and pure thoughts, comes to immediately realize that I am the Highest and most Superior Being in this universe. He who truly realize this fact knows all that is to be known in this world and thus he worships Me, with all his might and soul.
explanation: He who has a clear vision and is constantly occupied with only divine and pure thoughts, comes to immediately realize that I am the Highest and most Superior Being in this universe. He who truly realize this fact knows all that is to be known in this world and thus he worships Me, with all his might and soul.

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच । अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ...
hindi: श्रीभगवान् बोले —–भय का सर्वथा अभाव, अन्त:करण की पूर्ण निर्मलता, तत्व ज्ञान के लिये ध्यान योग में निरन्तर दृढ. स्थिति और सात्विक दान, इन्द्रियों का दमन, भगवान्, देवता और गुरुजनों की पूजा तथा अग्निहोत्र आदि उत्तम कर्मों का आचरण एवं वेद शास्त्रों का पठन-पाठन तथा भगवान् के नाम और गुणों का कीर्तन, स्वधर्म पालन के लिये कष्ट सहन और शरीर तथा इन्द्रियों के सहित अन्त:करण की सरलता ।
english: All of the significant qualities in all beings such as: freedom from fear, purity of mind and heart, stability in knowledge and concentration, generosity in charity, self-control, sacrifice, constant study of the holy Scriptures, piousness and straightforwardness.
explanation: All of the significant qualities in all beings such as: freedom from fear, purity of mind and heart, stability in knowledge and concentration, generosity in charity, self-control, sacrifice, constant study of the holy Scriptures, piousness and straightforwardness.

sanskrit: अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम् । दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥ १६.२ ॥
hindi: मन, वाणी और शरीर किसी प्रकार भी किसी को कष्ट न देना, यथार्थ और प्रिय भाषण, अपना अपकार करने वाले पर भी क्रोध का न होना, कर्मों में कर्तापन के अभिमान का त्याग, अन्त:करण की उपरति अर्थात् चित्त की चञ्चलता का अभाव, किसी की भी निन्दादि न करना, सब भूत प्राणियों में हेतुरहित दमा, इन्द्रियों का विषयों के साथ संयोग होने पर भी उनमें आसक्ति का न होना कोमलता, लोक और शास्त्र से विरुद्भ आचरण में लज्जा और व्यर्थ चेष्टाओं का अभाव ।
english: …Non-violence, truth, freedom from anger, detachment from all things, peacefulness (with mind and self), restraint from finding faults with others, compassion towards all living beings, detachment from greedy craving, gentleness, modesty, and stability of the mind and emotions.
explanation: …Non-violence, truth, freedom from anger, detachment from all things, peacefulness (with mind and self), restraint from finding faults with others, compassion towards all living beings, detachment from greedy craving, gentleness, modesty, and stability of the mind and emotions.

sanskrit: तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता । भवन्ति संपदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥ १६.३ ॥
hindi: तेज, क्षमा, धैर्य, बाहर की शुद्भि एवं किसी में भी शत्रु भाव का न होना और अपने में पूज्यता के अभिमान का अभाव —–ये सब तो हे अर्जुन ! दैवी सम्पदा को लेकर उत्पन्न हुए पुरुष के लक्षण हैं ।
english: Vigour, an attitude of forgiveness towards others, courage, purity, goodwill, towards others, and freedom from pride. All of these, dear Arjuna, are considered by Me to be the great characteristics of a man who possesses a divine nature and has come into this world from heaven.
explanation: Vigour, an attitude of forgiveness towards others, courage, purity, goodwill, towards others, and freedom from pride. All of these, dear Arjuna, are considered by Me to be the great characteristics of a man who possesses a divine nature and has come into this world from heaven.

sanskrit: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च । अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ संपदमासुरीम् ॥ १६.४ ॥
hindi: हे पार्थ ! दम्भ, घमण्ड और अभिमान तथा क्रोध, कठोरता और अज्ञान भी —-ये सब आसुरी सम्पदा को लेकर उत्पन्न हुए पुरुष के लक्षण हैं ।
english: Characteristics such as deceitfulness, arrogance, excessive pride, anger, harshness, rudeness and ignorance are the makings of a man who has been born on to this earth from fiery hell.
explanation: Characteristics such as deceitfulness, arrogance, excessive pride, anger, harshness, rudeness and ignorance are the makings of a man who has been born on to this earth from fiery hell.

sanskrit: दैवी संपद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता । मा शुचः संपदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥ १६.५ ॥
hindi: दैवी-सम्पदा मुक्त्ति के लिये और आसुरी-सम्पदा बाँधने के लिये मानी गयी है । इसलिये हे अर्जुन ! तू शोक मत कर ; क्योंकि तू दैवी-सम्पदा को लेकर उत्पन्न हुआ है ।
english: The qualities that exist within a person with a divine nature, leads that person to a blissful freedom and liberation from this world and all its material objects. However the evil traits that are found in a person with a demonic nature, blinds that person to this world and to the sensual objects it contains. But grieve not, beloved Devotee of Mine, you have been born with a divine personality and your final and ultimate destination is heaven.
explanation: The qualities that exist within a person with a divine nature, leads that person to a blissful freedom and liberation from this world and all its material objects. However the evil traits that are found in a person with a demonic nature, blinds that person to this world and to the sensual objects it contains. But grieve not, beloved Devotee of Mine, you have been born with a divine personality and your final and ultimate destination is heaven.

sanskrit: द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन्दैव आसुर एव च । दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे शृणु ॥ १६.६ ॥
hindi: हे अर्जुन ! इस लोक में भूतों की सृष्टि यानी मनुष्य समुदाय दो ही प्रकार का है, एक तो दैवी प्रकृति वाला, दूसरा आसुरी प्रकृति वाला । उनमे से दैवी प्रकृति वाला तो विस्तार पूर्वक कहा गया, अब तू आसुरी प्रकृति वाले मनुष्य समुदाय को भी विस्तार पूर्वक मुझ से सुन ।
english: There are basically two types of beings in this world O Arjuna. The first type is known to have a pure, pious and divine nature. The second however, has a demonic and evil nature. I have explained the divine nature to you in great length, O Arjuna. Now listen dear friend . as I describe the nature of the corrupt and evil and evil-doers in this world.
explanation: There are basically two types of beings in this world O Arjuna. The first type is known to have a pure, pious and divine nature. The second however, has a demonic and evil nature. I have explained the divine nature to you in great length, O Arjuna. Now listen dear friend . as I describe the nature of the corrupt and evil and evil-doers in this world.

sanskrit: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः । न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते ॥ १६.७ ॥
hindi: आसुर स्वभाव वाले मनुष्य प्रवृत्ति और निवृत्ति —इन दोनों को ही नहीं जानते । इसलिये उनमें न तो बाहर-भीतर की शुद्भि है, न श्रेष्ट आचरण है और न सत्य भाषण ही है ।
english: Evil men O Partha. do not know the difference between what should and should not be done. Neither purity nor good conduct, nor even a single sign of truth exists in their hearts.
explanation: Evil men O Partha. do not know the difference between what should and should not be done. Neither purity nor good conduct, nor even a single sign of truth exists in their hearts.

sanskrit: असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम् । अपरस्परसंभूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम् ॥ १६.८ ॥
